title.zh: 卷八十三 薛宣朱博傳
title.en: Volume 83: Xue Xuan and Zhu Bo
title.raw: 卷八十三 薛宣朱博傳 Volume 83: Xue Xuan and Zhu Bo

sentences.zh: 卷八十三 薛宣硃博傳第五十三

translations.literal: Volume 83: Biographies of Xue Xuan and Zhu Bo, Number 53.
translations.idiomatic: Volume 83: Biographies of Xue Xuan and Zhu Bo (chapter fifty-three).

sentences.zh: 薛宣字贛君，東海郯人也。

translations.literal: Xue Xuan, courtesy name Gan Jun, was a man of Tan in Donghai.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan, whose courtesy name was Gan Jun, came from Tan in Donghai Commandery.

sentences.zh: 少為廷尉書佐、都船獄吏。

translations.literal: In youth he served as a clerk to the commandant of justice and as a jailer in the capital-ships prison.
translations.idiomatic: While young he worked as a clerk under the commandant of justice and as a keeper in the capital-ships jail.

sentences.zh: 後以大司農斗食屬察廉，補不其丞。

translations.literal: Later, as a dou-shi subordinate of the grand minister of agriculture, he was nominated incorrupt and was appointed assistant magistrate of Buqi.
translations.idiomatic: Later, while attached to the grand minister of agriculture on a dou-shi stipend, he earned an incorrupt nomination and was posted as assistant magistrate of Buqi.

sentences.zh: 琅邪太守趙貢行縣，見宣，甚說其能。

translations.literal: Governor Zhao Gong of Langye was inspecting the counties; when he saw Xuan he was greatly pleased with his ability.
translations.idiomatic: When the governor of Langye, Zhao Gong, toured his districts and met Xue Xuan, he was so impressed by his talent that he took him along.

sentences.zh: 從宣歷行屬縣，還至府，令妻子與相見，戒曰：「贛君至丞相，我兩子亦中丞相史。」

translations.literal: He had Xuan accompany him through the subordinate counties, and when they returned to headquarters he had his wife and children meet Xuan and warned them, "When Gan Jun reaches the chancellorship, my two sons should also qualify as clerks in the chancellor's office."
translations.idiomatic: He brought Xue Xuan on his rounds of the dependent counties, and back at the yamen introduced him to his wife and children with the words: "If Gan Jun rises to be chancellor, I expect my sons to earn places among the chancellor's secretaries."

sentences.zh: 察宣廉，遷樂浪都尉丞。

translations.literal: Xuan was nominated incorrupt and was promoted to assistant commandant of Lelang.
translations.idiomatic: Nominated as incorrupt, Xue Xuan was promoted to assistant commandant of Lelang.

sentences.zh: 幽州刺史舉茂材，為宛句令。

translations.literal: The inspector of Youzhou nominated him as flourishing talent, and he became magistrate of Yuanju.
translations.idiomatic: The Youzhou regional inspector recommended him as flourishing talent, and he was appointed magistrate of Yuanju.

sentences.zh: 大將軍王鳳聞其能，薦宣為長安令，治果有名，以明習文法詔補御史中丞。

translations.literal: Grand General Wang Feng heard of his ability, recommended Xuan as magistrate of Chang'an; his administration indeed won renown, and because he was expert in statutes he was ordered to fill the post of vice-imperial secretary.
translations.idiomatic: When Grand General Wang Feng heard of his reputation, he recommended Xue Xuan for magistrate of Chang'an. His governance made a name, and his mastery of the code earned him an appointment as vice-imperial secretary.

sentences.zh: 是時，成帝初即位，宣為中丞，執法殿中，外總部刺史，上疏曰：「陛下至德仁厚，哀閔元元，躬有日仄之勞，而亡佚豫之樂，允執聖道，刑罰惟中，然而嘉氣尚凝，陰陽不和，是臣下未稱，而聖化獨有不洽者也。

translations.literal: At that time Emperor Cheng had just ascended the throne; Xuan was vice-imperial secretary, enforcing the law within the palace halls and overseeing the regional inspectors in the outer administration. He submitted a memorial: "Your Majesty's virtue is supremely benevolent; you pity the common people, personally toil past the slanting sun yet have no leisure for ease, truly holding fast to the sage's way so that punishments hit the mean—yet auspicious qi still congeals and yin and yang are not in harmony. That means your ministers have not matched their charge, and sagely transformation alone remains imperfect."
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Cheng had only just taken the throne. As vice-imperial secretary, Xue Xuan upheld the law inside the halls and coordinated the regional inspectors outside. He wrote: "Your Majesty's kindness is boundless: you grieve for the people, labor past sunset without thought of rest, and hold true to the sage's path so penalties stay even—yet good omens remain scarce and yin and yang stay out of tune. The fault lies with officials who fall short of your example, not with your own transforming power."

sentences.zh: 臣竊伏思其一端，殆吏多苛政，政教煩碎，大率咎在部刺史，或不循守條職，舉錯各以其意，多與郡縣事，至開私門，聽讒佞，以求吏民過失，譴呵及細微，責義不量力。

translations.literal: Your servant has privately pondered one aspect: probably the clerks practice harsh government, and policy and instruction are vexatious and petty; for the most part the blame lies with the regional inspectors—some do not keep to their statutory duties but raise and lower matters each according to private whim, meddle excessively in county affairs, open doors to favoritism, listen to slander, seek petty faults among clerks and people, rebuke trifles, and demand righteousness beyond what strength allows.
translations.idiomatic: I believe one root of the trouble is oppressive clerks and fussy regulations, and above all the regional inspectors: some ignore their proper brief, act on whim, meddle in county business, welcome backdoor influence, heed malicious talk, hunt for trivial offenses, and demand standards no one can meet.

sentences.zh: 郡縣相迫促，亦內相刻，流至眾庶。

translations.literal: Commanderies and counties press one another in turn, and within they are mutually harsh, until the evil reaches the common people.
translations.idiomatic: The pressure cascades from commandery to county and inward among the staff until ordinary folk bear the brunt.

sentences.zh: 是故鄉黨闕於嘉賓之歡，九族忘其親親之恩，飲食周急之厚彌衰，送往勞來之禮不行。

translations.literal: Therefore in the villages the joy of welcoming guests fails, the nine agnates forget the kindness of kin, the generosity of sharing food in emergencies grows thin, and the courtesies of seeing off and welcoming travelers are not practiced.
translations.idiomatic: Villages lose the pleasure of hospitality, kin forget how to treat one another as family, mutual aid in hardship withers, and the simple courtesies of travel and welcome fall away.

sentences.zh: 夫人道不通，則陰陽否隔，和氣不興，未必不由此也。

translations.literal: When the human way does not flow through, yin and yang are blocked apart and harmonious qi does not rise—perhaps it is not unrelated to this.
translations.idiomatic: When human relations are blocked, yin and yang cannot mingle and harmonious qi cannot gather—and this may well be the cause.

sentences.zh: 《詩》云：『民之失德，乾餱以愆。』

translations.literal: The Odes says, "When the people lose their virtue, it is over dried rations that they quarrel."
translations.idiomatic: The Classic of Odes says, "When the people abandon virtue, they bicker even over a crust of dry food."

sentences.zh: 鄙語曰：『苛政不親，煩苦傷恩。』

translations.literal: A vulgar saying runs, "Harsh rule drives away affection; vexation and toil wound kindness."
translations.idiomatic: A proverb warns: harsh rule destroys trust; petty harassment eats away at good will.

sentences.zh: 方刺史奏事時，宜明申敕，使昭然知本朝之要務。

translations.literal: When the inspectors report on their duties, you should clearly charge them so they plainly know this court's priorities.
translations.idiomatic: When inspectors come to report, Your Majesty should spell out your orders so they understand what the dynasty expects first of all.

sentences.zh: 臣愚不知治道，唯明主察焉。」

translations.literal: Your servant is foolish and does not know the way of governance; only may the enlightened ruler examine this."
translations.idiomatic: I am too dull to prescribe policy; I leave that to your sage judgment."

sentences.zh: 上嘉納之。

translations.literal: The sovereign praised and accepted it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved the memorial and acted on it.

sentences.zh: 宣數言政事便宜，舉奏部刺史郡國二千石，所貶退稱進，白黑分明，繇是知名。

translations.literal: Xuan repeatedly spoke on administrative conveniences, memorializing to impeach regional inspectors and commandery and kingdom officials at two-thousand-dan rank; whom he demoted or advanced was plainly marked black and white, and thereby he became famous.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan often proposed practical reforms and impeached regional inspectors and two-thousand-dan officials; his promotions and demotions were never murky, and his reputation spread.

sentences.zh: 出為臨淮太守，政教大行。

translations.literal: He went out as governor of Linhuai, and policy and instruction were greatly carried out.
translations.idiomatic: As governor of Linhuai he spread instruction and good order on a broad scale.

sentences.zh: 會陳留郡有大賊廢亂，上徙宣為陳留太守，盜賊禁止，吏民敬其威信。

translations.literal: It happened that Chenliu commandery had great banditry and ruinous disorder; the sovereign transferred Xuan to be governor of Chenliu; banditry was stopped, and clerks and people respected his awe-inspiring trustworthiness.
translations.idiomatic: When Chenliu was overrun by large outlaw bands, the court moved Xue Xuan there as governor; he stamped out the raiders, and officials and commoners alike deferred to his authority.

sentences.zh: 入守左馮翊，滿歲稱職為真。

translations.literal: He entered to act as guardian of Left Fengyi, and after a full year of being rated competent he was confirmed in the post.
translations.idiomatic: He took provisional charge of Left Fengyi, and after a full year's probation rated competent he received the substantive appointment.

sentences.zh: 始高陵令楊湛、櫟陽令謝游皆貪猾不遜，持郡短長，前二千石數案不能竟。

translations.literal: At first Magistrate Yang Zhan of Gaoling and Magistrate Xie You of Liyang were both greedy, crafty, and insubordinate; they held the commandery's weak points and several previous two-thousand-dan governors had investigated them without finishing the case.
translations.idiomatic: Magistrate Yang Zhan of Gaoling and Magistrate Xie You of Liyang had long been corrupt, slippery, and defiant; they had leverage over the commandery, and successive governors had tried and failed to bring them down.

sentences.zh: 及宣視事，詣府謁，宣設酒飯與相對，接待甚備。

translations.literal: When Xuan took office, they came to headquarters to pay respects; Xuan set out wine and food and sat opposite them, receiving them with full courtesy.
translations.idiomatic: When Xue Xuan assumed office, both men reported to the yamen. He entertained them with wine and food and treated them with elaborate courtesy.

sentences.zh: 已而陰求其罪臧，具得所受取。

translations.literal: Afterward he secretly sought their crimes of corruption and fully obtained what they had received and taken.
translations.idiomatic: Then he quietly gathered evidence of their embezzlement until he had every bribe accounted for.

sentences.zh: 宣察湛有改節敬宣之效，乃手自牒書，條其奸臧，封與湛曰：「吏民條言君如牒，或議以為疑於主守盜。

translations.literal: Xuan observed that Zhan showed signs of changing his conduct and respecting Xuan; he therefore personally drafted a memorandum listing his illicit takings, sealed it, and gave it to Zhan, saying, "Clerks and people have itemized charges against you as on this slip; some discussion holds that it verges on embezzlement by an official in custody of public funds.
translations.idiomatic: Seeing that Yang Zhan might yet reform and defer to him, Xue Xuan drew up a private memorandum detailing his corruption, sealed it, and handed it over with the message: "Petitions from clerks and people match this list; some advisers think the sums approach the crime of a custodial official stealing state property.

sentences.zh: 馮翊敬重令，又念十金法重，不忍相暴章。

translations.literal: As guardian of Fengyi I respect a magistrate's office, and I also recall that the law on ten jin of gold is heavy; I cannot bear to expose you publicly.
translations.idiomatic: As your superior I honor the magistrate's dignity, and the statute on ten catties of gold is severe; I would rather not drag your shame into the open.

sentences.zh: 故密以手書相曉，欲君自圖進退，可復伸眉於後。

translations.literal: Therefore I secretly use this handwritten note to make it clear, wishing you to plan your own advance or retreat so that afterward you may again hold your head high.
translations.idiomatic: I write privately so you may choose your own course and still hold your head high afterward.

sentences.zh: 即無其事，復封還記，得為君分明之。」

translations.literal: If there is no such matter, seal and return this note, and I can clear the matter for you in detail."
translations.idiomatic: If any of this is wrong, seal the note back to me and I will sort the facts for you."

sentences.zh: 湛自知罪臧皆應記，而宣辭語溫潤，無傷害意。

translations.literal: Zhan knew himself that the crimes of corruption all matched the note, yet Xuan's wording was mild and warm, without intent to harm.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Zhan knew every charge was true, yet the tone stayed gentle, without malice.

sentences.zh: 湛即時解印綬付吏，為記謝宣，終無怨言。

translations.literal: Zhan immediately handed his seal and ribbon to the clerk, wrote a note thanking Xuan, and to the end had no resentful words.
translations.idiomatic: He surrendered his seal at once, sent a courteous note of thanks, and never spoke a word of bitterness.

sentences.zh: 而櫟陽令游自以大儒有名，輕宣。

translations.literal: But Magistrate You of Liyang, considering himself a great Confucian with a reputation, slighted Xuan.
translations.idiomatic: Magistrate Xie You of Liyang, fancying himself a famous scholar, treated Xue Xuan with contempt.

sentences.zh: 宣獨移書顯，責之曰：「告櫟陽令：吏民言令治行煩苛，適罰作使千人以上；

translations.literal: Xuan alone sent an open circular and reproached him, saying, "To the magistrate of Liyang: clerks and people report that your administration is vexatious and harsh, that you have improperly assigned more than a thousand men to penal labor;
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan issued a blunt public letter: "To the magistrate of Liyang: complaints say your rule is cruel and petty, that you have pressed more than a thousand men into corvée labor beyond what the law allows;

sentences.zh: 賊取錢財數十萬，給為非法；

translations.literal: that you have seized several hundred thousand in cash and supplied it for unlawful purposes;
translations.idiomatic: that you have extorted several hundred thousand in cash for illicit ends;

sentences.zh: 賣買聽任富吏，賈數不可知。

translations.literal: that in buying and selling you let rich clerks do as they please so that the sums cannot be known;
translations.idiomatic: that you let wealthy clerks rig purchases and sales so the figures cannot be traced;

sentences.zh: 證驗以明白，欲遣吏考案，恐負舉者，恥辱儒士，故使掾平鐫令。

translations.literal: the evidence is clear; I wished to send clerks to investigate but feared shaming the one who nominated you and disgracing a Confucian, therefore I have had clerk Ping hand you this inscribed order.
translations.idiomatic: the proof is plain. I might have sent investigators but dreaded embarrassing your patron and humiliating a man of letters, so I sent clerk Ping to deliver this written order instead.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：『陳力就列，不能者止。』

translations.literal: Confucius said, "Display your strength and take your place; if you cannot, stop."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "Take a post only if you can fill it; otherwise step down."

sentences.zh: 令詳思之，方調守。」

translations.literal: Consider this carefully; you are about to be transferred to a guardian post."
translations.idiomatic: Think it through—you are about to be rotated into a caretaker appointment."

sentences.zh: 游得檄，亦解印綬去。

translations.literal: You received the summons and likewise handed over seal and ribbon and departed.
translations.idiomatic: Xie You read the summons, resigned his seal, and left.

sentences.zh: 又頻陽縣北當上郡、西河，為數郡湊，多盜賊。

translations.literal: Also, north of Pinyang county lay Shangjun and Xihe; it was a junction of several commanderies and had many bandits.
translations.idiomatic: North of Pinyang lay the border with Shangjun and Xihe—a crossroads of several commanderies where bandits swarmed.

sentences.zh: 其令平陵薛恭本縣孝者，功次稍遷，未嘗治民，職不辦。

translations.literal: Its magistrate, Xue Gong of Pingling, was a filial candidate of his home county; he had risen by seniority of merit but had never governed the people and could not manage his duties.
translations.idiomatic: The magistrate there, Xue Gong of Pingling, was a local filial nominee promoted by seniority; he had never run a county and could not cope.

sentences.zh: 而栗邑縣小，辟在山中，民謹樸易治。

translations.literal: But Liyi county was small, secluded in the hills, and the people were cautious and simple and easy to govern.
translations.idiomatic: Liyi, by contrast, was a small, hill-bound county whose people were tractable and plain.

sentences.zh: 令巨鹿尹賞久郡用事吏，為樓煩長，舉茂材，遷在栗。

translations.literal: Magistrate Yin Shang of Julu was a longtime clerk experienced in commandery business; he had been chief of Loufan, was nominated flourishing talent, and had been moved to Liyi.
translations.idiomatic: Yin Shang of Julu, a veteran commandery clerk, had served as chief of Loufan, earned a flourishing-talent nomination, and been posted to Liyi.

sentences.zh: 宣即以令奏賞與恭換縣。

translations.literal: Xuan thereupon by order memorialized to have Shang and Gong exchange counties.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan memorialized to swap the two magistrates.

sentences.zh: 二人視事數月，而兩縣皆治。

translations.literal: The two took office for several months, and both counties were well governed.
translations.idiomatic: Within months both counties were in good order.

sentences.zh: 宣因移書勞勉之曰：「昔孟公綽優於趙魏而不宜滕薛，故或以德顯，或以功舉，『君子之道，焉可憮也！』

translations.literal: Xuan therefore sent a written message to encourage them, saying, "Of old Meng Gongchuo was more than a match for Zhao and Wei but was not suited to Teng and Xue—thus some are distinguished by virtue and some lifted by achievement. The way of the gentleman—how can it be cramped!"
translations.idiomatic: He wrote to encourage them: "Meng Gongchuo excelled in great states yet faltered in small ones—some shine through virtue, others through deeds. A gentleman's path admits more than one shape."

sentences.zh: 屬縣各有賢君，馮翊垂拱蒙成。

translations.literal: Each dependent county now has a worthy magistrate; the guardian of Fengyi may fold his hands and inherit success.
translations.idiomatic: With able magistrates in every county, the guardian of Fengyi can rule with folded hands.

sentences.zh: 願勉所職，卒功業。」

translations.literal: Strive in your offices and complete your merit."
translations.idiomatic: Press on in your duties and finish the work you have begun."

sentences.zh: 宣得郡中吏民罪名，輒召告其縣長吏，使自行罰。

translations.literal: Whenever Xuan obtained charges against clerks or people in the commandery, he would summon and inform their county magistrates or chiefs and have them impose punishment themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever he learned of wrongdoing among officials or commoners, he notified their county magistrates and left them to punish their own subordinates.

sentences.zh: 曉曰：「府所以不自發舉者，不欲代縣治，奪賢令長名也。」

translations.literal: He explained, "The reason the headquarters does not itself initiate prosecution is that I do not wish to replace the counties in governance and steal the good name of worthy magistrates and chiefs."
translations.idiomatic: He explained, "I do not prosecute from the yamen because I will not usurp county government or rob worthy magistrates of their credit."

sentences.zh: 長吏莫不喜懼，免冠謝宣歸恩受戒者。

translations.literal: The chiefs none failed to feel both joy and fear; removing their caps they thanked Xuan, returned grace, and received admonition.
translations.idiomatic: Every magistrate felt gratitude and awe, doffed his cap to thank him, and accepted his lesson.

sentences.zh: 宣為吏賞罰明，用法平而必行，所居皆有條教可紀，多仁恕愛利。

translations.literal: As a clerk Xuan's rewards and punishments were clear, his use of the law even and certain to be carried out; wherever he served there were regulations worth recording, and he was broadly humane, forgiving, and devoted to benefit.
translations.idiomatic: In office his rewards and penalties were clear, his law even-handed and firm; every place he served gained workable rules, and he was remembered for mercy and practical kindness.

sentences.zh: 池陽令舉廉吏獄掾王立，府未及召，聞立受囚家錢。

translations.literal: The magistrate of Chiyang nominated the incorrupt jail clerk Wang Li of his county; before headquarters had summoned him, it was heard that Li had accepted money from a prisoner's family.
translations.idiomatic: The magistrate of Chiyang nominated jail clerk Wang Li for an incorrupt award; before the summons arrived, word spread that Wang had taken money from a prisoner's kin.

sentences.zh: 宣責讓縣，縣案驗獄掾，乃其妻獨受系者錢萬六千，受之再宿，獄掾實不知。

translations.literal: Xuan rebuked the county; the county investigated the jail clerk, and it turned out that only his wife had alone accepted sixteen thousand from someone in custody, receiving it for two nights, while the jail clerk truly did not know.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan rebuked the county; their inquiry showed that Wang Li's wife alone had accepted sixteen thousand cash from a detainee's family for two nights, while Wang himself knew nothing.

sentences.zh: 掾慚恐自殺。

translations.literal: The clerk, ashamed and frightened, killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: The clerk, mortified and terrified, took his own life.

sentences.zh: 宣聞之，移書池陽曰：「縣所舉廉吏獄掾王立，傢俬受賕，而立不知，殺身以自明，立誠廉士，甚可閔惜！

translations.literal: When Xuan heard, he sent a message to Chiyang saying, "The incorrupt jail clerk Wang Li whom the county nominated—members of his household privately took a bribe while Li did not know; he killed himself to clear his name—Li was truly an incorrupt gentleman, greatly to be pitied!
translations.idiomatic: Hearing this, Xue Xuan wrote to Chiyang: "Wang Li was nominated as incorrupt, yet someone in his household secretly took a bribe without his knowledge; he died to prove his innocence—a truly honest man, and deeply to be mourned!

sentences.zh: 其以府決曹掾書立之柩，以顯其魂。

translations.literal: Let the headquarters decision-clerk inscribe his name on Li's bier to display his spirit.
translations.idiomatic: Let the headquarters decision clerk inscribe his name on the coffin to honor his spirit.

sentences.zh: 府掾史素與立相知者，皆予送葬。」

translations.literal: All headquarters clerks and secretaries who were ordinarily acquainted with Li shall attend the funeral."
translations.idiomatic: Every yamen clerk who knew him should join the funeral cortege."

sentences.zh: 及日至休吏，賊曹掾張扶獨不肯休，坐曹治事。

translations.literal: When it came to the solstice day of rest for clerks, the bandit-clerk Zhang Fu alone refused to rest, sitting in his bureau handling business.
translations.idiomatic: On the statutory rest day for yamen staff, Zhang Fu of the bandit bureau alone stayed at his desk.

sentences.zh: 宣出教曰：「蓋禮貫和，人道尚通。

translations.literal: Xuan issued instruction: "In general ritual values harmony, and the human way esteems communication.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan issued a notice: "Ritual prizes harmony; human relations need breathing room.

sentences.zh: 日至，吏以令休，所繇來久。

translations.literal: On the solstice clerks rest by order; the practice has come down a long time.
translations.idiomatic: The solstice holiday for clerks is ancient statute.

sentences.zh: 曹雖有公職事，家亦望私恩意。

translations.literal: Though the bureau has public duties, the family also hopes for private kindness and consideration.
translations.idiomatic: Even offices with public business should remember that families expect a little private kindness.

sentences.zh: 掾宜從眾，歸對妻子，設酒餚，請鄰里，一笑相樂，斯亦可矣！」

translations.literal: Clerks ought to follow the multitude, return to face wives and children, set out wine and dishes, invite neighbors, and laugh together in pleasure—this too is acceptable!"
translations.idiomatic: Go home to your wives, pour wine, invite the neighbors, and enjoy an evening—that is enough!"

sentences.zh: 扶慚愧。

translations.literal: Fu felt ashamed.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Fu was abashed.

sentences.zh: 官屬善之。

translations.literal: The official staff praised this.
translations.idiomatic: The staff admired the gesture.

sentences.zh: 宣為人好威儀，進止雍容，甚可觀也。

translations.literal: Xuan as a person loved imposing deportment; advancing and withdrawing he was dignified and very fine to behold.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan loved ceremony; his bearing in motion was stately and impressive.

sentences.zh: 性密靜有思，思省吏職，求其便安。

translations.literal: By nature he was quiet and thoughtful; he reflected on clerical duties and sought their convenience and ease.
translations.idiomatic: By temperament he was calm and reflective, always rethinking how clerks could work more smoothly.

sentences.zh: 下至財用筆研，皆為設方略，利用而省費。

translations.literal: Down to supplies, cash, brush, and inkstone he devised plans so as to be useful and save expense.
translations.idiomatic: He even planned the budget for brushes and ink so the office ran leaner without hardship.

sentences.zh: 吏民稱之，郡中清靜。

translations.literal: Clerks and people praised him, and the commandery was clear and quiet.
translations.idiomatic: Officials and commoners praised him, and the commandery stayed orderly and calm.

sentences.zh: 遷為少府，共張職辦。

translations.literal: He was promoted to privy treasurer, and for the joint arrangements of his duties he was competent.
translations.idiomatic: Promoted to privy treasurer, he handled court provisioning without a hitch.

sentences.zh: 月餘，御史大夫於永卒，谷永上疏曰：

translations.literal: After more than a month, Imperial Secretary Yu Yong died, and Gu Yong submitted a memorial:
translations.idiomatic: A month later Imperial Secretary Yu Yong died, and Gu Yong presented a memorial:

sentences.zh: 帝王之德莫大於知人，知人則百僚任職，天工不曠。

translations.literal: Of the virtue of emperors and kings none is greater than knowing men; knowing men, then the hundred offices fill their posts and the work of heaven is not left vacant.
translations.idiomatic: No royal virtue surpasses knowing how to pick men: when the right men fill every post, Heaven's work is never neglected.

sentences.zh: 故皋陶曰：「知人則哲，能官人。」

translations.literal: Therefore Gao Yao said, "To know men is wisdom; to be able to assign offices is ability."
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao said, "To know men is wisdom; to place them rightly is statesmanship."

sentences.zh: 御史大夫內承本朝之風化，外佐丞相統理天下，任重職大，非庸材所能堪。

translations.literal: The imperial secretary within receives the transforming influence of this court and without assists the chancellor in ordering all under heaven; the burden is weighty and the office great—not something a mediocre talent can sustain.
translations.idiomatic: The imperial secretary carries the court's ethos inside the palace and helps the chancellor govern the realm; the burden is too heavy for mediocrity.

sentences.zh: 今當選於群卿，以充其缺。

translations.literal: Now one must be chosen from the host of ministers to fill the vacancy.
translations.idiomatic: The vacancy must be filled from among the senior ministers.

sentences.zh: 得其人則萬姓欣喜，百僚說服；

translations.literal: If the right man is obtained, the myriad surnames will rejoice and the hundred offices will be pleased and convinced;
translations.idiomatic: Choose well and the people rejoice and every bureau falls into line;

sentences.zh: 不得其人則大職墮□，王功不興。

translations.literal: if the wrong man is obtained, the great office will collapse and royal achievements will not rise.
translations.idiomatic: choose poorly and the great office collapses while the kingly enterprise stalls.

sentences.zh: 虞帝之明，在茲一舉，可不致詳！

translations.literal: In the clarity of Emperor Shun it hung on this one act—can you fail to be thorough!
translations.idiomatic: Even Shun's wisdom turned on a single appointment—how can you be less than meticulous?

sentences.zh: 竊見少府宣，材茂行潔，達於從政，前為御史中丞，執憲轂下，不吐剛茹柔，舉錯時當；

translations.literal: Your servant has observed Privy Treasurer Xuan: his talents are flourishing and his conduct pure; he is thorough in governance; formerly as vice-imperial secretary he upheld the law under the chariot hub, neither spitting out the hard nor swallowing the soft, and his promotions and demotions were timely;
translations.idiomatic: I have watched Privy Treasurer Xue Xuan: able, upright, and seasoned in administration. As vice-imperial secretary he enforced the law at the capital without flinching from power or crushing the weak, and every personnel move was timely;

sentences.zh: 出守臨淮、陳留，二郡稱治；

translations.literal: going out to guard Linhuai and Chenliu, both commanderies praised his rule;
translations.idiomatic: as governor of Linhuai and Chenliu both regions called him well governed;

sentences.zh: 為左馮翊，崇教養善，威德並行，眾職修理，奸軌絕息，辭訟者歷年不至丞相府，赦後余盜賊什分三輔之一。

translations.literal: as guardian of Left Fengyi he honored instruction and nurtured the good; awe-inspiring virtue and kindness went together; the multitude of duties were put in order, wicked plots ceased, litigants for years did not reach the chancellor's office, and after an amnesty the remaining bandits were only one-third those of the other two metropolitan areas.
translations.idiomatic: as guardian of Left Fengyi he taught goodness and combined authority with kindness until lawsuits for years never reached the chancellor and, after an amnesty, bandits in his jurisdiction fell to a third of the rest of the capital region.

sentences.zh: 功效卓爾，自左內史初置以來未嘗有也。

translations.literal: The merit was outstanding; since the first establishment of the inner guardian of the left there has never been its like.
translations.idiomatic: Such results are unmatched since the office of left inner guardian was created.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「如有所譽，其有所試。」

translations.literal: Confucius said, "If I praise someone, I have put him to the test."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "If I praise a man, I have tested him."

sentences.zh: 宣考績功課，簡在兩府，不敢過稱以奸欺誣之罪。

translations.literal: Xuan's examination of merit and performance is on record in both bureaus; your servant dares not overpraise and incur the crime of deceitful slander.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan's performance reviews stand in the files of both chancellery and censorate; I dare not flatter him and risk the charge of false praise.

sentences.zh: 臣聞賢材莫大於治人，宣已有效。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that among worthy talents none is greater than governing others, and Xuan already has proven effect.
translations.idiomatic: The greatest talent is the ability to govern others, and Xue Xuan has already proved he has it.

sentences.zh: 其法律任廷尉有餘，經術文雅足以謀王體，斷國論；

translations.literal: As to the law, he could fill the post of commandant of justice with surplus; in classical learning and cultured refinement he is sufficient to deliberate on the kingly person and decide state policy;
translations.idiomatic: He knows the statutes well enough to be commandant of justice and has enough classical culture to counsel on the person of the ruler and on national policy;

sentences.zh: 身兼數器，有「退食自公」之節。

translations.literal: his person combines several instruments and he has the integrity of "withdrawing from the meal and returning to public duty."
translations.idiomatic: he combines several kinds of excellence and shows the integrity praised in the ode about returning from court with unstained honor.

sentences.zh: 宣無私黨遊說之助，臣恐陛下忽於《羔羊》之詩，捨公實之臣，任華虛之譽，是用越職，陳宣行能，唯陛下留神考察。

translations.literal: Xuan has no faction of private hangers-on to speak for him; your servant fears lest Your Majesty overlook the ode "The Lamb" and discard a minister of solid achievement for empty reputation—therefore I overstep my office to set forth Xuan's conduct and ability; only may Your Majesty fix your attention and investigate.
translations.idiomatic: He has no clique to tout him. I fear Your Majesty might neglect the lesson of "The Lamb" and set aside a man of proven weight for one with hollow fame—so I overstep my rank to commend Xue Xuan's record and beg you to examine it closely.

sentences.zh: 上然之，遂以宣為御史大夫。

translations.literal: The sovereign approved and thereupon made Xuan imperial secretary.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor agreed and appointed Xue Xuan imperial secretary.

sentences.zh: 數月，代張禹為丞相，封高陽侯，食邑千戶。

translations.literal: After several months he replaced Zhang Yu as chancellor, was enfeoffed as Marquis of Gaoyang, and had a fief of a thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: Months later he succeeded Zhang Yu as chancellor, was enfeoffed as Marquis of Gaoyang with a thousand-household fief.

sentences.zh: 宣除趙貢兩子為史。

translations.literal: Xuan appointed Zhao Gong's two sons as clerks.
translations.idiomatic: He took Zhao Gong's two sons onto his staff as secretaries.

sentences.zh: 貢者，趙廣漢之兄子也，為吏亦有能名。

translations.literal: Gong was the nephew of Zhao Guanghan and as a clerk also had a reputation for ability.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Gong was Zhao Guanghan's nephew and had himself earned a name as a capable official.

sentences.zh: 宣為相，府辭訟例不滿萬錢不為移書，後皆遵用薛侯故事。

translations.literal: When Xuan was chancellor, the rule at headquarters was not to issue formal documents for lawsuits under ten thousand cash; afterward all followed the precedent of Marquis Xue.
translations.idiomatic: As chancellor he ruled that the yamen would not transmit suits involving less than ten thousand cash; later offices copied Marquis Xue's precedent.

sentences.zh: 然官屬譏其煩碎無大體，不稱賢也。

translations.literal: Yet his subordinates ridiculed him as fussy and lacking large perspective, not matching worthiness.
translations.idiomatic: His staff mocked him for pettifogging and missing the big picture, and denied that he was truly worthy.

sentences.zh: 時天子好儒雅，宣經術又淺，上亦輕焉。

translations.literal: At that time the Son of Heaven loved Confucian elegance; Xuan's classical learning was also shallow, and the sovereign likewise slighted him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor favored literary Confucians, and Xue Xuan's scholarship was thin, so the throne cooled toward him as well.

sentences.zh: 久之，廣漢郡盜賊群起，丞相、御史遣掾史逐捕不能克。

translations.literal: After a long time, in Guanghan commandery bandits rose in swarms; the chancellor and imperial secretary sent clerks and secretaries to pursue and capture them but could not overcome them.
translations.idiomatic: Years later Guanghan erupted in outlaw bands; chancellor and censor sent yamen runners after them without success.

sentences.zh: 上乃拜河東都尉趙護為廣漢太守，以軍法從事。

translations.literal: The sovereign then appointed Commandant of the Guard Zhao Hu of Hedong as governor of Guanghan, to conduct affairs by military law.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor named Zhao Hu, commandant of the guard in Hedong, governor of Guanghan with authority to use military methods.

sentences.zh: 數月，斬其渠帥鄭躬，降者數千人，乃平。

translations.literal: After several months he beheaded their chieftain Zheng Gong; those who surrendered were several thousand persons, and then it was pacified.
translations.idiomatic: Within months he executed their leader Zheng Gong; thousands surrendered, and the region was quiet.

sentences.zh: 會邛成太后崩，喪事倉卒，吏賦斂以趨辦。

translations.literal: It happened that the Empress Dowager of Qiongcheng died; the funeral affairs were rushed, and clerks levied exactions to hurry completion.
translations.idiomatic: Then Empress Dowager Qiongcheng died; the funeral was rushed, and officials squeezed the people to get everything done in haste.

sentences.zh: 其後上聞之，以過丞相、御史，遂冊免宣曰：「君為丞相，出入六年，忠孝之行，率先百僚，朕無聞焉。

translations.literal: Afterward the sovereign heard of it, blamed the chancellor and imperial secretary, and then by edict dismissed Xuan, saying, "You have been chancellor, going in and out six years; in conduct of loyalty and filial piety, taking the lead among the hundred officials, We have heard nothing of you.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor learned what had happened, he blamed the chancellor and the censor, then issued an edict dismissing Xue Xuan: "Six years you have been chancellor, yet I have heard nothing of you leading the bureaucracy in loyalty or filial duty."

sentences.zh: 朕既不明，變異數見，歲比不登，倉廩空虛，百姓饑饉，流離道路，疾疫死者以萬數，人至相食，盜賊並興，群職曠廢，是朕之不德而股肱不良也。

translations.literal: We are already not enlightened; prodigies have repeatedly appeared; harvests have year by year failed to ripen; granaries are empty; the hundred surnames suffer famine, drift homeless on the roads, and those dead of pestilence number in the tens of thousands, until men reach the point of eating one another; bandits arise together; the host of offices lie vacant and abandoned—this is Our lack of virtue and the poor quality of Our arms and thighs."
translations.idiomatic: "I am a dull ruler: omens multiply, the crops fail year after year, the granaries are empty, the people starve and wander the roads, plague carries off tens of thousands until they turn to cannibalism, bandits rise everywhere, and government grinds to a halt. That reflects my lack of virtue and the failure of my chief ministers."

sentences.zh: 乃者廣漢群盜橫恣，殘賊吏民，朕惻然傷之，數以問君，君對輒不如其實。

translations.literal: Recently the bandit hordes of Guanghan ran riot and slaughtered clerks and people; We were grieved for them and repeatedly questioned you, yet each time you answered, it was not as the reality was."
translations.idiomatic: "When the Guanghan outlaws ran wild and butchered officials and commoners, I grieved for the victims and questioned you again and again, and every answer fell short of the truth."

sentences.zh: 西州隔絕，幾不為郡。

translations.literal: The western region was cut off and almost ceased to be a commandery.
translations.idiomatic: The western circuits were isolated and nearly lost to the empire.

sentences.zh: 三輔賦斂無度，酷吏並緣為奸，侵擾百姓，詔君案驗，復無慾得事實之意。

translations.literal: In the three metropolitan areas exactions were without measure; cruel clerks together seized the chance for villainy and encroached on the hundred surnames; We ordered you to investigate, yet again you had no mind to wish to obtain the facts of the matter."
translations.idiomatic: "In the capital region tax levies knew no limit, harsh underlings turned the chaos to profit, and the people suffered—yet when I ordered you to investigate, you showed no interest in learning what really happened."

sentences.zh: 九卿以下，咸承風指，同時陷於謾欺之辜，咎繇君焉！

translations.literal: From the nine ministers downward, all caught your tone and at the same time fell into the guilt of deceit and fraud—the blame proceeds from you!
translations.idiomatic: "From the nine ministers down, everyone took your cue and drifted into lies and evasion. The fault is yours."

sentences.zh: 有司法君領職解嫚，開謾欺之路，傷薄風化，無以帥示四方。

translations.literal: Someone impeached you for slackness in directing your duties, opening the road to deceit and fraud, injuring and thinning customs and transformation, with no way to lead and show the four quarters."
translations.idiomatic: "You are charged with loose supervision that invited fraud, corroded public morals, and left you unfit to set an example for the realm."

sentences.zh: 不忍致君於理，其上丞相、高陽侯印綬，罷歸。」

translations.literal: We cannot bear to bring you to the law; present the seals and ribbons of chancellor and Marquis of Gaoyang and retire."
translations.idiomatic: "I will not drag you through a criminal trial. Surrender the seals of the chancellorship and of the Marquis of Gaoyang and go home."

sentences.zh: 初，宣為丞相，而翟方進為司直。

translations.literal: At first, when Xuan was chancellor, Zhai Fangjin was rectifier.
translations.idiomatic: When Xue Xuan first became chancellor, Zhai Fangjin held the post of rectifier.

sentences.zh: 宣知方進名儒，有宰相器，深結厚焉。

translations.literal: Xuan knew Fangjin was a famous Confucian with the capacity of a chancellor and bound himself to him in deep friendship.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan knew Zhai Fangjin was a noted scholar with chancellor timber and cultivated him warmly.

sentences.zh: 後方進竟代為丞相，思宣舊恩，宣免後二歲，薦宣明習文法，練國制度，前所坐過薄，可復進用。

translations.literal: Later Fangjin in the end replaced him as chancellor; remembering Xuan's old kindness, two years after Xuan was dismissed he recommended Xuan as expert in statutes and practiced in state institutions, that the earlier offense was slight and he could again be advanced and employed.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhai Fangjin eventually succeeded him, he remembered that debt; two years after Xue Xuan's dismissal he recommended him as a master of statute and state institutions whose earlier fault had been small and who deserved another chance.

sentences.zh: 上征宣復爵高陽侯，加寵特進，位次師安昌侯，給事中，視尚書事。

translations.literal: The sovereign summoned Xuan and restored his noble rank as Marquis of Gaoyang, added favor as specially advanced, rank next to Tutor Marquis of Anchang, gave attendance within the palace, and charged him to oversee the affairs of the secretariat.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor recalled Xue Xuan, restored his title as Marquis of Gaoyang, advanced him to specially advanced rank just below Tutor Shi of Anchang, gave him a palace attendant post, and assigned him to oversee secretariat business.

sentences.zh: 宣復尊重。

translations.literal: Xuan again was honored and weighty.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan regained prestige and influence.

sentences.zh: 任政數年，後坐善定陵侯淳于長罷就第。

translations.literal: After holding office several years, later he was charged for friendship with Marquis of Dingling Chunyu Zhang and dismissed to his mansion.
translations.idiomatic: Some years later he fell because he had been close to Chunyu Zhang, Marquis of Dingling, and was sent home.

sentences.zh: 初，宣有兩弟，明、修：明至南陽太守；

translations.literal: At first Xuan had two younger brothers, Ming and Xiu: Ming reached governor of Nanyang;
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan had two younger brothers, Ming and Xiu; Ming rose to be governor of Nanyang;

sentences.zh: 修歷郡守、京兆尹、少府，善交接，得州裡之稱。

translations.literal: Xiu successively served as commandery governor, metropolitan governor, and privy treasurer; he was good at social connections and won praise in his district.
translations.idiomatic: Xiu served as commandery governor, metropolitan governor, and privy treasurer; he was skilled at networking and popular in his home region.

sentences.zh: 後母常從修居官。

translations.literal: His stepmother always followed Xiu to his official posts.
translations.idiomatic: Their stepmother habitually lived with Xiu wherever he held office.

sentences.zh: 宣為丞相時，修為臨菑令，宣迎後母，修不遣。

translations.literal: When Xuan was chancellor, Xiu was magistrate of Linzi; Xuan welcomed the stepmother, but Xiu would not send her.
translations.idiomatic: While Xue Xuan was chancellor, Xiu was magistrate of Linzi; when Xuan asked to bring the stepmother to the capital, Xiu refused to let her go.

sentences.zh: 後母病死，修去官持服。

translations.literal: The stepmother died of illness; Xiu left office to observe mourning.
translations.idiomatic: When the stepmother died, Xiu resigned to observe the mourning rites.

sentences.zh: 宣謂修三年服少能行之者，兄弟相駁不可，修遂竟服，繇是兄弟不和。

translations.literal: Xuan said that for the three-year mourning few could practice it; the brothers disputed one another and could not agree; Xiu in the end completed the full mourning, and thereby the brothers were not in harmony.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan argued that few men truly kept the full three-year mourning and tried to talk his brother out of it; Xiu insisted on completing the full term anyway, and the two fell out.

sentences.zh: 事不有司，御史中丞眾等奏：「況朝臣，父故宰相，再封列侯，不相敕丞化，而骨肉相疑，疑咸受修言以謗毀宣。

translations.literal: The matter not having gone to the proper offices, Vice-Imperial Secretary Zhong and others submitted: "Kuang is a court minister; his father was a former chancellor, twice enfeoffed as marquis; he did not mutually instruct and assist transformation, yet flesh and bone suspected one another; it is suspected that Xian received Xiu's words to slander and destroy Xuan.
translations.idiomatic: The case never reached the regular judiciary. Vice-Imperial Secretary Zhong and colleagues memorialized: "Xue Kuang is a court official whose father was twice a chancellor and a marquis, yet instead of setting a moral example he let kin turn on kin; Shen Xian is thought to have taken Xue Xiu's stories and used them to defame Xue Xuan."

sentences.zh: 咸所言皆宣行跡，眾人所共見，公家所宜聞。

translations.literal: What Xian spoke was all Xuan's conduct and traces, what the multitude together saw and what the public house ought to have heard.
translations.idiomatic: What Shen Xian said was common knowledge about Xue Xuan's conduct—facts the court should already have heard.

sentences.zh: 況知咸給事中，恐為司隸舉奏宣，而公令明等迫切宮闕，要遮創戮近臣於大道人眾中，欲以隔塞聰明，杜絕論議之端。

translations.literal: Kuang knew Xian was in attendance within the palace and feared he would impeach Xuan as metropolitan superintendent; he openly ordered Ming and others to press close to the palace gates, intercept and wound a near minister in the middle of the great road among the crowd, wishing thereby to block clear hearing and cut off the beginnings of discussion.
translations.idiomatic: Kuang knew Shen Xian served in palace attendance and feared he would impeach Xue Xuan once metropolitan superintendent; he therefore sent Ming and others to crowd the palace approaches, waylay a senior minister in broad daylight in a public street, hoping to deafen the throne to criticism."

sentences.zh: 桀黠無所畏忌，萬眾言雚嘩，流聞四方，不與凡民忿怒爭鬥者同。

translations.literal: Wicked and cunning, without anything they feared or shunned, the myriad crowd clamored and hubbubbed, flowing rumor to the four quarters—not the same as common people angry and fighting.
translations.idiomatic: It was a brazen, calculated act that set the capital roaring—not a common brawl among angry townsfolk."

sentences.zh: 臣聞敬近臣，為近主也。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that to respect near ministers is to be near the ruler.
translations.idiomatic: To assault a minister of the inner court is to strike at the ruler himself."

sentences.zh: 禮，下公門，式路馬，君畜產且猶敬之。

translations.literal: By ritual, when descending from a duke's gate one pats the road horse; the ruler's domestic animals are yet respected.
translations.idiomatic: Ritual requires even a duke's gate horse to be treated with respect—how much more a man at the emperor's side?"

sentences.zh: 《春秋》之義，意惡功遂，不免於誅，上浸之源不可長也，況首為惡，明手傷，功意俱惡，皆大不敬。

translations.literal: The meaning of the Spring and Autumn is: when the intent is evil though the deed succeeds, execution is not avoided; the spring of upward seeping must not be lengthened—how much more when one is first in evil, clearly wounds with the hand, and both deed and intent are evil—all is great irreverence.
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn Annals condemn evil intent even when the crime "succeeds"; the first trickle of contempt for authority must not be tolerated—especially when the blow is premeditated, delivered with the fist, and both motive and act are outrageous."

sentences.zh: 明當以重論，及況皆棄市。」

translations.literal: Ming should be judged heavily; Kuang and the rest should be exposed in the market."
translations.idiomatic: Ming deserves the full penalty; Kuang and his accomplices should die in the public square."

sentences.zh: 廷尉直以為：「律曰『斗以刃傷人，完為城旦，其賊加罪一等，與謀者同罪。』

translations.literal: Commandant of justice Zhi held: "The statute says, 'In a brawl, if one wounds another with a blade, let him be intact and sentenced to wall dawn; if it is malicious wounding, add one degree to the crime, the same guilt as conspirators.'"
translations.idiomatic: Commandant of justice Zhi argued: "The code says that in a brawl a cut with a blade earns wall-dawn labor at intact status, malicious wounding adds one degree, and accomplices share the principal's guilt."

sentences.zh: 詔書無以詆欺成罪。

translations.literal: Edicts do not make slander alone a completed crime.
translations.idiomatic: Imperial edicts do not treat mere defamation as a completed capital offense."

sentences.zh: 傳曰：『遇人不以義而見□者，與□人之罪鈞，惡不直也。』

translations.literal: The tradition says, 'When one meets a person not in accordance with rightness and is wronged by him, to strike the wrongdoer is equal guilt—evil is not being straight.'"
translations.idiomatic: The exegetical tradition says that when someone wrongs you without justice, striking back matches the wrongdoer's guilt—because the response is not upright."

sentences.zh: 咸厚善修，而數稱宣惡，流聞不誼，不可謂直。

translations.literal: Xian was thickly friendly with Xiu and repeatedly proclaimed Xuan's evil; rumor spread of impropriety—this cannot be called straight.
translations.idiomatic: Shen Xian was Xiu's intimate and kept retailing scandal about Xue Xuan; the talk that spread was hardly honorable—so his conduct was not "straight" either."

sentences.zh: 況以故傷咸，計謀已定，後聞置司隸，因前謀而趣明，非以恐咸為司隸故造謀也。

translations.literal: Kuang for an old grudge wounded Xian; the plot was already set when later he heard a metropolitan superintendent was appointed; he therefore pressed forward with the earlier plan through Ming—it was not that he newly devised the plot because he feared Xian would become metropolitan superintendent.
translations.idiomatic: Kuang struck Shen Xian over an old feud; the ambush was already planned before a metropolitan superintendent was named, and Ming merely carried it out—Kuang did not hatch the scheme only because he feared Shen Xian's new appointment."

sentences.zh: 本爭私變，雖於掖門外傷咸道中，與凡民爭鬥無異。

translations.literal: Originally they disputed private matters; although they wounded Xian outside the Ye Gate on the road, it differs nothing from common people fighting.
translations.idiomatic: At bottom it was a private quarrel; though the blow fell outside the Ye Gate, it was still a street fight among private citizens."

sentences.zh: 殺人者死，傷人者刑，古今之通道，三代所不易也。

translations.literal: Those who kill die, those who wound suffer punishment—the thoroughfare from antiquity to the present, what the three ages have not altered.
translations.idiomatic: Killers die and wounders are punished—that rule has held since high antiquity and the three dynasties never changed it."

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：『必也正名。』

translations.literal: Confucius said, "There must be correction of names."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "The first task is to set names right."

sentences.zh: 名不正，則至於刑罰不中；

translations.literal: If names are not correct, then punishments do not hit the mean;
translations.idiomatic: If names are wrong, penalties cannot be even;

sentences.zh: 刑罰不中，而民無所錯手足。

translations.literal: if punishments do not hit the mean, the people have nowhere to put hand or foot.
translations.idiomatic: when penalties are uneven, the people lose all bearings."

sentences.zh: 今以況為首惡，明手傷為大不敬，公私無差。

translations.literal: Now to treat Kuang as chief in evil and Ming's hand-wounding as great irreverence makes no distinction between public and private.
translations.idiomatic: To brand Kuang as the arch-villain and Ming's punch as "great irreverence" collapses public crime with private brawling."

sentences.zh: 《春秋》之義，原心定罪。

translations.literal: The meaning of the Spring and Autumn is: examine the heart to fix the crime.
translations.idiomatic: The Annals teach us to weigh intent when fixing guilt."

sentences.zh: 原況以父見謗發忿怒，無它大惡。

translations.literal: Examining Kuang, he was moved by anger because his father was slandered; he had no other great evil.
translations.idiomatic: Kuang acted from rage at slander against his father; there was no deeper treason."

sentences.zh: 加詆欺，輯小過成大辟，陷死刑，違明詔，恐非法意，不可施行。

translations.literal: To add slander charges, gather petty faults into a capital crime, trap him in death penalty, and violate a clear edict—We fear it is not the intent of the law and cannot be carried out.
translations.idiomatic: Piling on defamation, inflating a petty affray into a capital case, and ignoring an explicit amnesty would violate the spirit of the statutes."

sentences.zh: 聖王不以怒增刑。

translations.literal: A sage king does not increase punishment because of anger.
translations.idiomatic: A sage ruler does not sharpen sentences in a fit of temper."

sentences.zh: 明當以賊傷人不直，況與謀者皆爵減完為城旦。」

translations.literal: Ming should be sentenced for malicious wounding without rightness; Kuang and the conspirators should all have noble rank reduced and intact wall dawn."
translations.idiomatic: Ming should answer for malicious battery; Kuang and his fellow plotters should lose one noble rank and serve wall-dawn labor at intact status."

sentences.zh: 上以問公卿議臣。

translations.literal: The sovereign thereupon asked the dukes and ministers and deliberating officials.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor referred the question to his ministers and policy advisers.

sentences.zh: 丞相孔光、大司空師丹以中丞議是，自將軍以下至博士、議郎皆是廷尉。

translations.literal: Chancellor Kong Guang and Grand Minister of Works Shi Dan held the vice-imperial secretary's opinion correct; from generals downward to erudits and deliberation gentlemen, all sided with the commandant of justice.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Kong Guang and Grand Minister of Works Shi Dan agreed with the censor's bench; from the generals down through the academicians everyone backed the commandant of justice.

sentences.zh: 況竟減罪一等，徙敦煌。

translations.literal: Kuang in the end had his crime reduced one degree and was banished to Dunhuang.
translations.idiomatic: Kuang's sentence was commuted one step and he was exiled to Dunhuang.

sentences.zh: 宣坐免為庶人，歸故郡，卒於家。

translations.literal: Xuan by the case was dismissed to commoner status, returned to his home commandery, and died at home.
translations.idiomatic: Stripped of office for his connection to the affair, Xue Xuan went home to his native commandery and died there.

sentences.zh: 宣子惠亦至二千石。

translations.literal: Xuan's son Hui also reached two-thousand-dan rank.
translations.idiomatic: His son Xue Hui likewise rose to two-thousand-dan rank.

sentences.zh: 始惠為彭城令，宣從臨淮遷至陳留，過其縣，橋梁、郵亭不修。

translations.literal: At first when Hui was magistrate of Pengcheng, Xuan moved from Linhuai to Chenliu and passed through his county; bridges and post stations were not repaired.
translations.idiomatic: When Xue Hui was magistrate of Pengcheng, his father passed through on transfer from Linhuai to Chenliu and noticed bridges and relay stations left unrepaired.

sentences.zh: 宣心知惠不能，留彭城數日，案行捨中，處置什器，觀視園菜，終不問惠以吏事。

translations.literal: Xuan in his heart knew Hui was not capable; he stayed in Pengcheng several days, inspected the lodging, arranged utensils, and looked over the garden vegetables, but to the end did not ask Hui about clerkly affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan knew his son was not up to the job; he lingered a few days, inspected the guest quarters, rearranged the household gear, and toured the kitchen garden—yet never mentioned administration.

sentences.zh: 惠自知治縣不稱宣意，遣門下掾送宣至陳留，令掾進見，自從其所問宣不教戒惠吏職之意。

translations.literal: Hui himself knew that governing the county did not match Xuan's intent; he sent his gate clerk to escort Xuan to Chenliu, had the clerk advance to audience, and himself followed wherever he asked Xuan the intent of not instructing and warning Hui about clerkly duties.
translations.idiomatic: Hui guessed he had failed his father's expectations; he sent a gate clerk to accompany Xuan toward Chenliu, then had the clerk ask, in his stead, why his father offered no word of guidance on magisterial duty.

sentences.zh: 宣笑曰：「吏道以法令為師，可問而知。

translations.literal: Xuan laughed and said, "The way of clerks takes statutes and ordinances as teacher; it can be known by asking.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan laughed and said, "The clerk's art is spelled out in the code—read it and you will know.

sentences.zh: 及能與不能，自有資材，何可學也？」

translations.literal: As to ability or inability, each has his native endowment—how can it be learned?"
translations.idiomatic: Whether you have the knack is a gift of nature; that cannot be taught."

sentences.zh: 眾人傳稱，以宣言為然。

translations.literal: The multitude passed the story around and held Xuan's words correct.
translations.idiomatic: Onlookers repeated the story and agreed the father was right.

sentences.zh: 初，宣復封為侯時，妻死，而敬武長公主寡居，上令宣尚焉。

translations.literal: At first, when Xuan was again enfeoffed as marquis, his wife died, while Princess Jingwu of reverent martialness lived a widow's life; the sovereign ordered Xuan to marry her.
translations.idiomatic: When Xue Xuan regained his marquisate, his wife had died, and the widowed Princess Jingwu was available; the emperor ordered him to marry her.

sentences.zh: 及宣免歸故郡，公主留京師。

translations.literal: When Xuan was dismissed and returned to his home commandery, the princess remained in the capital.
translations.idiomatic: After he was stripped of office and sent home, the princess stayed in Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 後宣卒，主上書願還宣葬延陵，奏可。

translations.literal: Later when Xuan died, the princess submitted a memorial wishing to return Xuan for burial at Yanling; the memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: When Xue Xuan died, she petitioned to bury him at Yanling, and the throne consented.

sentences.zh: 況私從敦煌歸長安，會赦，因留與主私亂。

translations.literal: Kuang privately returned from Dunhuang to Chang'an; it happened that there was an amnesty; he therefore stayed and had secret disorder with the princess.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Kuang slipped back from Dunhuang during a general amnesty and began a clandestine affair with the princess.

sentences.zh: 哀帝外家丁、傅貴，主附事之，而疏王氏。

translations.literal: The outer kin of Emperor Ai, Ding and Fu, were noble; the princess attached to them and kept the Wang clan at distance.
translations.idiomatic: When the Ding and Fu consorts rose under Emperor Ai, the princess sided with them and turned against the Wangs.

sentences.zh: 元始中，莽自尊為安漢公，主又出言非莽。

translations.literal: In the Yuanshi era Mang on his own honored himself as Duke of Anhan; the princess again spoke against Mang.
translations.idiomatic: During Yuanshi, Wang Mang styled himself Duke of Anhan, and the princess openly criticized him.

sentences.zh: 而況與呂寬相善，及寬事覺時，莽並治況，發揚其罪，使使者以太皇太后詔賜主藥。

translations.literal: But Kuang was on good terms with Lü Kuan; when Kuan's affair was discovered, Mang together prosecuted Kuang, broadcast his crimes, and had envoys with the Grand Empress Dowager's edict bestow poison on the princess.
translations.idiomatic: Kuang was close to Lü Kuan; when Lü's plot came to light, Wang Mang prosecuted Kuang, publicized his guilt, and sent agents with an edict from the grand empress dowager ordering the princess to take poison.

sentences.zh: 主怒曰：「劉氏孤弱，王氏擅朝，排擠宗室，且嫂何與取妹披抉其閨門而殺之？」

translations.literal: The princess angrily said, "The Liu house is orphaned and weak while the Wang house monopolizes the court, squeezing out the imperial kin—and what has a sister-in-law to do with seizing a younger sister, prying open her women's quarters, and killing her?"
translations.idiomatic: She raged: "The Liu are powerless while the Wangs run the state and hound the imperial clan—and what business is it of a sister-in-law to break into a younger sister's private rooms and murder her?"

sentences.zh: 使者迫守主，遂飲藥死。

translations.literal: The envoys pressed and guarded the princess; she thereupon drank the poison and died.
translations.idiomatic: The agents surrounded her until she swallowed the drug and died.

sentences.zh: 況梟首於市。

translations.literal: Kuang's head was displayed in the market.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Kuang's head was hung in the marketplace.

sentences.zh: 白太后雲主暴病薨。

translations.literal: He reported to the empress dowager that the princess had suddenly died of illness.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang told the empress dowager she had died of a sudden illness.

sentences.zh: 太后欲臨其喪，莽固爭，乃止。

translations.literal: The empress dowager wished to attend her funeral; Mang firmly disputed it, and then it stopped.
translations.idiomatic: She wished to attend the obsequies, but Wang Mang blocked her until she gave up the idea.

sentences.zh: 硃博字子元，杜陵人也。

translations.literal: Zhu Bo, courtesy name Ziyuan, was a man of Duling.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo, courtesy name Ziyuan, came from Duling.

sentences.zh: 家貧，少時給事縣為亭長，好客少年，捕搏敢行。

translations.literal: His family was poor; in youth he served the county as village head, loved hosting young bravos, and in arresting and fighting dared to act.
translations.idiomatic: Poor as a boy, he served as a village head, entertained young swordsmen, and made arrests without flinching.

sentences.zh: 稍遷為功曹，伉俠好交，隨從士大夫，不避風雨。

translations.literal: He was gradually promoted to merit clerk; he was high-spirited and chivalrous, fond of friendship, and followed gentlemen and grandees through wind and rain.
translations.idiomatic: Promoted to merit clerk, he cultivated a swaggering, loyal style, followed scholars and magnates through every weather, and loved a wide circle of friends.

sentences.zh: 是時，前將軍望之子蕭育，御史大夫萬年子陳咸以公卿子著材知名，博皆友之矣。

translations.literal: At that time Xiao Yu, son of the former general Wang, and Chen Xian, son of Imperial Secretary Wan, were famous as sons of dukes and ministers of outstanding talent; Bo was already friends with them.
translations.idiomatic: In those days Xiao Yu, son of General Wang, and Chen Xian, son of Imperial Secretary Wan, were celebrated heirs of the elite, and Zhu Bo numbered among their companions.

sentences.zh: 時，諸陵縣屬太常，博以太常掾察廉，補安陵丞。

translations.literal: At that time the mausoleum counties belonged to the chamberlain of ceremonials; Bo as a clerk to the chamberlain was nominated incorrupt and was appointed assistant magistrate of Anling.
translations.idiomatic: The imperial tomb districts fell under the chamberlain of ceremonials; Zhu Bo, serving on that staff, won an incorrupt nomination and became assistant magistrate of Anling.

sentences.zh: 後去官入京兆，歷曹史列掾。

translations.literal: Later he left office and entered the metropolitan area, passing through clerkships and column clerkships.
translations.idiomatic: He later resigned, moved to the capital district, and worked his way through a series of yamen posts.

sentences.zh: 出為督郵書掾，所部職辦，郡中稱之。

translations.literal: He went out as clerk for visiting inspector and letter clerk; the jurisdiction's duties were handled, and the commandery praised him.
translations.idiomatic: As visiting inspector and correspondence clerk he cleared his circuit's backlog and won the commandery's praise.

sentences.zh: 而陳咸為御史中丞，坐漏洩省中語下獄。

translations.literal: But Chen Xian was vice-imperial secretary; he sat for leaking language from within the palace and was cast into prison.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Xian became vice-imperial secretary but landed in jail for leaking palace secrets.

sentences.zh: 博去吏，間步至廷尉中，候伺咸事。

translations.literal: Bo left his clerkship, walked stealthily to the commandant of justice's office, and waited to spy on Xian's case.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo quit his post, slipped into the commandant of justice compound, and watched Chen Xian's trial.

sentences.zh: 咸掠治困篤，博詐得為醫人獄，得見咸，具知其所坐罪。

translations.literal: Xian was tortured in investigation and was in dire straits; Bo by deception became a physician in the prison, was able to see Xian, and fully learned what crimes he sat for.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Xian was racked until he was near death; Zhu Bo posed as a prison doctor, gained access, and learned every count against him.

sentences.zh: 博出獄，又變性名，為咸驗治數百，卒免咸死罪。

translations.literal: Bo left the prison, also changed surname and given name, and for Xian ran several hundred examinations of evidence, in the end exempting Xian from the death penalty.
translations.idiomatic: He then changed his name, filed hundreds of affidavits on Chen Xian's behalf, and finally spared him execution.

sentences.zh: 咸得論出，而博以此顯名，為郡功曹。

translations.literal: Xian was sentenced and released, and Bo thereby became famous and was merit clerk of the commandery.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Xian walked free, Zhu Bo's reputation was made and he became the commandery merit clerk.

sentences.zh: 博本武吏，不更文法，及為刺史行部，吏民數百人遮道自言，官寺盡滿。

translations.literal: Bo was fundamentally a military clerk and had not passed through literary law; when he became a regional inspector touring his division, several hundred clerks and people blocked the road to speak for themselves, and government offices were all full.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo was a rough soldier-clerk with no training in the code; on his first inspection tour hundreds of petitioners mobbed the road and filled every office.

sentences.zh: 從事白請且留此縣錄見諸自言者，事畢乃發，欲以觀試博。

translations.literal: The attendant clerk reported, asking to stay in this county for the moment, record and interview all who spoke for themselves, and when matters were finished then set out, wishing thereby to observe and test Bo.
translations.idiomatic: His aide suggested they delay in that county, hear every petitioner, and then move on—intending to test the new inspector.

sentences.zh: 博心知之，告外趣駕。

translations.literal: Bo knew it in his heart and told those outside to hurry the carriage.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo saw through the ploy and ordered his carriage readied at once.

sentences.zh: 既白駕辦，博出就車見自言者，使從事明敕告吏民：「欲言縣丞尉者，刺史不察黃綬，各自詣郡。

translations.literal: When it had been reported that the carriage was ready, Bo came out, mounted the carriage, and met those who spoke for themselves; he had the attendant clerk clearly instruct clerks and people: "Those who wish to speak of county assistants or captains—the regional inspector does not investigate yellow-ribbon officials; each go to the commandery yourselves.
translations.idiomatic: Once the team was harnessed, he rode out to the crowd and had his aide announce: "Complaints against county assistants or captains—this inspector does not hear cases against yellow-sash officials; take those to the commandery yourselves.

sentences.zh: 欲言二千石墨綬長吏者，使者行部還，詣治所。

translations.literal: Those who wish to speak of two-thousand-dan black-ribbon chiefs, when the envoy finishes touring the division, go to the headquarters."
translations.idiomatic: Charges against two-thousand-dan black-sash magistrates may be lodged at my yamen when I finish the circuit."

sentences.zh: 其民為吏所冤，及言盜賊辭訟事，各使屬其部從事。」

translations.literal: As for the people wronged by clerks, and matters of banditry, litigation, and suits, each shall report to the attendant clerk of his section."
translations.idiomatic: Petitions about malfeasant clerks, robbery, or lawsuits go to the sectional aide for each circuit."

sentences.zh: 博駐車決遣，四五百人皆罷去，如神。

translations.literal: Bo halted the carriage, decided and sent them away; four or five hundred persons all dispersed, as if by spirits.
translations.idiomatic: He disposed of four or five hundred cases from his chariot as if by magic, and the crowd melted away.

sentences.zh: 吏民大驚，不意博應事變乃至於此。

translations.literal: Clerks and people were greatly startled, not expecting Bo's handling of affairs to reach this point.
translations.idiomatic: Officials and commoners were stunned that he could clear such a mob so decisively.

sentences.zh: 後博徐問，果老從事教民聚會。

translations.literal: Later Bo slowly inquired and indeed it was an old attendant who had taught the people to gather.
translations.idiomatic: Later inquiry showed a senior clerk had coached the demonstration.

sentences.zh: 博殺此吏，州郡畏博威嚴。

translations.literal: Bo executed this clerk, and commanderies and prefectures feared Bo's awe-inspiring dignity.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo had that clerk put to death, and every commandery learned to fear him.

sentences.zh: 徙為并州刺史、護漕都尉，遷琅邪太守。

translations.literal: He was transferred to be regional inspector of Bingzhou, commandant who escorts transport, and promoted to governor of Langye.
translations.idiomatic: He moved on to regional inspector of Bingzhou, escort commandant for the grain fleets, and then governor of Langye.

sentences.zh: 齊舒緩養名，博新視事，右曹掾史皆移病臥。

translations.literal: Qi was relaxed and slow, cultivating reputation; when Bo had newly taken office, all clerks of the right bureau reported illness and lay down.
translations.idiomatic: Qi culture was languid and reputation-conscious; when Zhu Bo arrived, every clerk in the right bureau called in sick.

sentences.zh: 博問其故，對言：「惶恐！

translations.literal: Bo asked the reason; they answered, "We are terrified!
translations.idiomatic: He demanded an explanation; they quavered, "We dare not presume—

sentences.zh: 故事二千石新到，輒遣吏存問致意，乃敢起就職。」

translations.literal: By precedent, when a new two-thousand-dan arrives, he always sends clerks with greetings and expressions of intent; only then do we dare rise and take office."
translations.idiomatic: whenever a new governor comes, custom requires him to send greetings before we return to duty."

sentences.zh: 博奮髯抵幾曰：「觀齊兒欲以此為俗邪！」

translations.literal: Bo ruffled his whiskers and struck the table, saying, "Do you Qi boys intend to make this the custom!"
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo swept his beard aside, slammed the desk, and roared, "Do you mean to make a ritual of laziness?"

sentences.zh: 乃召見諸曹史書佐及縣大吏，選視其可用者，出教置之。

translations.literal: Thereupon he summoned and met all bureau clerks, letter clerks, and county senior clerks, selected and looked over those who could be used, issued instructions and placed them.
translations.idiomatic: He summoned every yamen clerk, picked those worth keeping, and issued fresh assignments.

sentences.zh: 皆斥罷諸病吏，白巾走出府門。

translations.literal: He dismissed all the sick clerks; in white headcloths they ran out the headquarters gate.
translations.idiomatic: The malingerers were cashiered; they fled the yamen in white mourning cloth.

sentences.zh: 郡中大驚。

translations.literal: The commandery was greatly startled.
translations.idiomatic: The whole commandery was shaken.

sentences.zh: 頃之，門下掾贛遂耆老大儒，教授數百人，拜起舒遲。

translations.literal: Presently the gate clerk Gan Sui was an old great Confucian who taught several hundred disciples; his bows and risings were slow and delayed.
translations.idiomatic: Soon after, the gate clerk Gan Sui—a venerable scholar with hundreds of pupils—shuffled through his bows at a snail's pace.

sentences.zh: 博出教主簿：「贛老生不習吏禮，主簿且教拜起，閑習乃止。」

translations.literal: Bo issued instruction to the chief clerk: "The old gentleman Gan is not practiced in clerk ritual; let the chief clerk for the moment teach him bows and risings, and when he is practiced in leisure, then stop."
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo told his chief clerk, "Coach the old master in how to bow; drill him until he gets it right."

sentences.zh: 又敕功曹：「官屬多褒衣大□，不中節度，自今掾史衣皆令去地三寸。」

translations.literal: He also instructed the merit clerk: "The official staff mostly wear baggy robes and great sashes, not hitting measure and rule; from now on let clerk and secretary garments all be ordered three cun from the ground."
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the merit clerk: "Your men waddle about in billowing robes that break every regulation; from today every clerk's hem stays three cun clear of the ground."

sentences.zh: 博尤不愛諸生，所至郡輒罷去議曹，曰：「豈可復置謀曹邪！」

translations.literal: Bo especially did not love the literati; in every commandery he reached he dismissed the deliberation bureau, saying, "How can we again install a planning bureau!"
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo had little patience for scholars: in each commandery he abolished the deliberation staff, declaring, "I will not revive a separate 'strategy office.'"

sentences.zh: 文學儒吏時有奏記稱說云云，博見謂曰：「如太守漢吏，奉三尺律令以從事耳，亡奈生所言聖人道何也！

translations.literal: Literary Confucian clerks from time to time submitted memorials praising this and that; when Bo saw them he said to them, "Such as the governor being a Han clerk—he carries the three-chi statutes and ordinances to conduct affairs; what have I to do with what you gentlemen speak of the sage's way!
translations.idiomatic: When Confucian clerks filed ornate memorials, he cut them short: "I am a Han magistrate: my job is the code on this desk, not your disquisitions on the Way of the sages."

sentences.zh: 且持此道歸，堯、舜君出，為陳說之。」

translations.literal: Moreover take this doctrine home; when Yao and Shun appear as rulers, present it to them."
translations.idiomatic: Take that sermon home and save it for the day Yao and Shun walk the earth again."

sentences.zh: 其折逆人如此。

translations.literal: His manner of rebuffing people was like this.
translations.idiomatic: That was typical of how he brushed people aside.

sentences.zh: 視事數年，大改其俗，掾史禮節如夢、趙吏。

translations.literal: After several years in office he greatly changed their customs; clerk ritual and deportment became like clerks of Yan and Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Within a few years local habits had tightened until his yamen clerks carried themselves like the brisk officials of Yan and Zhao.

sentences.zh: 博治郡，常令屬縣各用其豪桀以為大吏，文武從宜。

translations.literal: When Bo governed a commandery, he constantly ordered dependent counties each to employ their local stalwarts as senior clerks, civil or military as fit.
translations.idiomatic: As governor he told each county to press its own leading men into service as chief aides, mixing civil and martial talent as the case required.

sentences.zh: 縣有劇賊及它非常，博輒移書以詭責之。

translations.literal: If a county had fierce bandits or other extraordinary crises, Bo would send a written order with sharp reproof.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever a county faced serious outlaws or emergencies, he fired off a stern circular demanding results.

sentences.zh: 其盡力有效，必加厚賞；

translations.literal: Those who exerted themselves with effect, he invariably rewarded generously;
translations.idiomatic: Men who produced arrests won rich rewards;

sentences.zh: 懷詐不稱，誅罰輒行。

translations.literal: those who harbored deceit and fell short, executions and beatings followed at once.
translations.idiomatic: those caught dissembling were punished immediately.

sentences.zh: 以是豪強慹服。

translations.literal: By this means the powerful clans were awed into submission.
translations.idiomatic: The great houses learned to obey.

sentences.zh: 姑幕縣有群輩八人報仇廷中，皆不得。

translations.literal: In Gumu county a band of eight took revenge in the county hall; none were caught.
translations.idiomatic: Eight men in Gumu took revenge inside the county hall, and none were caught.

sentences.zh: 長吏自系書言府，賊曹掾史自白請至姑幕。

translations.literal: The chief officer bound himself and wrote to headquarters; bandit-bureau clerks themselves reported, asking permission to go to Gumu.
translations.idiomatic: The county head sent a self-blaming petition to the yamen, and the bandit clerks begged to ride out to Gumu.

sentences.zh: 事留不出。

translations.literal: The matter was held up and did not go out.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo let the request sit.

sentences.zh: 功曹諸掾即皆自白，復不出。

translations.literal: The merit clerk and all secretaries thereupon reported themselves; again they did not go out.
translations.idiomatic: The merit clerk and aides renewed their plea; again he refused.

sentences.zh: 於是府丞詣閣，博乃見丕丞掾曰：「以為縣自有長吏，府未嘗與也，丞掾謂府當與之邪？」

translations.literal: Thereupon the commandery assistant governor came to the gate; Bo then met the magistrates' assistants and said, "I thought the county has its own chief officers—the headquarters has never been mixed in; do the assistants think the headquarters ought to be mixed in?"
translations.idiomatic: The commandery's assistant governor came to the gate, and Zhu Bo told the county aides: "Each county has its own magistrate. This office has never run your arrests—do you imagine the governor's yamen should do your job for you?"

sentences.zh: 閣下書佐入，博口占檄文曰：「府告姑幕令丞：言賊發不得，有書。

translations.literal: A letter clerk below the gate entered; Bo orally dictated a summons: "The headquarters informs the magistrate and assistant of Gumu: you report that the bandits were sent out and not taken—there is a letter.
translations.idiomatic: He dictated a summons for the gate clerk: "Tell Gumu's magistrate and assistant: you wrote that the robbers escaped capture.

sentences.zh: 檄到，令丞就職，游檄王卿力有餘，如律令！」

translations.literal: When the summons arrives, magistrate and assistant shall take their posts; roaming inspector Wang Qing has strength to spare—according to statute and ordinance!"
translations.idiomatic: When this order reaches you, resume duty and put patrol officer Wang Qing to work at full stretch—as the law demands."

sentences.zh: 王卿得敕惶怖，親屬失色，晝夜馳鶩，十餘日間捕得五人。

translations.literal: Wang Qing received the charge, terrified; kin changed color; he galloped day and night, and within ten-odd days captured five men.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Qing took the order in terror, roused his kin, and ran himself ragged until within two weeks five men were in custody.

sentences.zh: 博復移書曰：「王卿憂公甚效！

translations.literal: Bo again sent a message: "Wang Qing's anxiety for the public good was very effective!
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo followed with praise: "Wang Qing has served the public with remarkable zeal!

sentences.zh: 檄到，繼伐閱詣府。

translations.literal: When the summons arrives, Wang Qing shall carry his merit record and present it at headquarters.
translations.idiomatic: When this order reaches you, Wang Qing must bring his merit dossier to the yamen for review.

sentences.zh: 部掾以下亦可用，漸盡其餘矣。」

translations.literal: Clerks below the sectional rank may also be used; gradually exhaust the remainder."
translations.idiomatic: Sectional aides and below may be drafted as well—work through the rest of the gang in order."

sentences.zh: 其操持下，皆此類也。

translations.literal: His handling of subordinates was all of this kind.
translations.idiomatic: His management of underlings always ran along such lines.

sentences.zh: 以高弟入守左馮翊，滿歲為真。

translations.literal: With top rank he entered to act as guardian of Left Fengyi; after a full year he was confirmed in the post.
translations.idiomatic: Rated at the top of the list, he took provisional charge of Left Fengyi and received the substantive appointment a year later.

sentences.zh: 其治左馮翊，文理聰明殊不及薛宣，而多武譎，網絡張設，少愛利，敢誅殺。

translations.literal: In governing Left Fengyi his cultured pattern and keen intelligence fell far short of Xue Xuan's, but he used more military cunning, spread nets and snares, showed little kindness for profit, and dared to execute and kill.
translations.idiomatic: As guardian of Left Fengyi he lacked Xue Xuan's polish and subtlety but relied on military wiles, tight surveillance, scant mercy, and a readiness to kill.

sentences.zh: 然亦縱捨，時有大貸，下吏以此為盡力。

translations.literal: Yet he also relaxed and released, at times granting large indulgences; subordinates for this reason exerted themselves to the utmost.
translations.idiomatic: Yet he could also relent and grant sweeping pardons, which made his men fight all the harder for him.

sentences.zh: 長陵大姓尚方禁少時嘗盜人妻，見斫，創著其頰。

translations.literal: In Changling a great surname, Shangfang Jin, in youth had once stolen another's wife; he was hacked and the wound scarred his cheek.
translations.idiomatic: Shangfang Jin of Changling, a powerful local, had once abducted another man's wife in his youth and been slashed across the cheek for it.

sentences.zh: 府功曹受賂，白除禁調守尉。

translations.literal: The headquarters merit clerk took a bribe and reported to remove Jin and transfer him as assistant captain.
translations.idiomatic: The merit clerk took a payoff and had Jin quietly posted as assistant captain.

sentences.zh: 博聞知，以它事召見，視其面，果有瘢。

translations.literal: Bo heard and knew; on another pretext he summoned him for audience, looked at his face, and indeed there was a scar.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo heard of it, called Jin in on other business, and spotted the scar.

sentences.zh: 博辟左右問禁：「是何等創也？」

translations.literal: Bo dismissed those at his sides and asked Jin, "What sort of wound is this?"
translations.idiomatic: Sending attendants away, he asked, "What left that mark?"

sentences.zh: 禁自知情得，叩頭服狀。

translations.literal: Jin knew the facts had been got; he knocked his head and confessed the circumstances.
translations.idiomatic: Jin knew the game was up and confessed.

sentences.zh: 博笑曰：「丈夫固時有是。
