translations.literal: Bo laughed and said, "A grown man naturally sometimes has such things.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo laughed: "Every man has skeletons in his past.

sentences.zh: 馮翊欲灑卿恥，□拭用禁，能自效不？」

translations.literal: The guardian of Fengyi wishes to wash away your shame—will you serve if I use you and devote yourself?"
translations.idiomatic: I mean to wipe away your shame by employing you—will you serve me to the death?"

sentences.zh: 禁且喜且懼，對曰：「必死！」

translations.literal: Jin was both pleased and afraid; he answered, "I will surely die!"
translations.idiomatic: Jin answered, trembling with hope and fear, "To the death!"

sentences.zh: 博因敕禁：「毋得洩語，有便宜，輒記言。」

translations.literal: Bo thereupon charged Jin, "You must not leak words; whenever there is something advantageous, record and report."
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo swore him to secrecy and told him to pass on every useful scrap of intelligence.

sentences.zh: 因親信之以為耳目。

translations.literal: He therefore trusted him intimately and made him eyes and ears.
translations.idiomatic: He kept Jin close as an informant.

sentences.zh: 禁晨夜發起部中盜賊及它伏奸，有功效。

translations.literal: Jin by day and night stirred up bandits in the commandery and other hidden villains; there were meritorious results.
translations.idiomatic: Jin flushed out bandits and hidden criminals day and night with striking success.

sentences.zh: 博擢禁連守縣令。

translations.literal: Bo promoted Jin to serve in succession as magistrate of several counties.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo raised him in stages until he served as magistrate of several counties in turn.

sentences.zh: 久之，召見功曹，閉閣數責以禁等事，與筆札使自記，「積受取一錢以上，無得有所匿。

translations.literal: After a long time he summoned the merit clerk, closed the doors, and for some time rebuked him over Jin and the like matters; he gave him brush and slips to write himself, "All sums taken, down to one cash—nothing may be concealed.
translations.idiomatic: Later he shut the merit clerk in his office, berated him over the Jin affair, handed him brush and bamboo slips, and ordered: "List every bribe you ever took, down to a single cash—hide nothing.

sentences.zh: 欺謾半言，斷頭矣！」

translations.literal: If you deceive with half a word, you lose your head!"
translations.idiomatic: One lie and you lose your head."

sentences.zh: 功曹惶怖，具自疏奸臧，大小不敢隱。

translations.literal: The merit clerk was terrified and in full detail wrote out his wicked corruption, great and small, not daring to conceal.
translations.idiomatic: The clerk, shaking, wrote out every bribe large and small.

sentences.zh: 博知其對以實，乃令就席，受敕自改而已。

translations.literal: Bo knew his reply matched the facts; then he ordered him to take his mat, received his pledge to reform himself, and that was all.
translations.idiomatic: Satisfied it was true, Zhu Bo let him sit down, accepted his vow to mend his ways, and stopped there.

sentences.zh: 投刀使削所記，遣出就職。

translations.literal: He threw down a knife and had him scrape out what he had written, then sent him out to resume duty.
translations.idiomatic: He tossed him a knife to scrape the confession clean from the slips and sent him back to work.

sentences.zh: 功曹後常戰慄，不敢蹉跌，博遂成就之。

translations.literal: The merit clerk afterward constantly trembled and did not dare stumble; Bo thereupon completed his transformation.
translations.idiomatic: The clerk thereafter walked on eggshells and never slipped again—exactly the discipline Zhu Bo wanted.

sentences.zh: 遷為大司農。

translations.literal: He was promoted to grand minister of agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: He rose to grand minister of agriculture.

sentences.zh: 歲餘，坐小法，左遷犍為太守。

translations.literal: After a year and more he sat for a minor statute and was demoted left to governor of Qianwei.
translations.idiomatic: A year later a petty legal fault cost him his rank; he was demoted to governor of Qianwei.

sentences.zh: 先是，南蠻若兒數為寇盜，博厚結其昆弟，使為反間，襲殺之，郡中清。

translations.literal: Earlier the southern Man Ruo'er had repeatedly been bandit raiders; Bo thickly befriended his brothers, used them as double agents, attacked and killed him, and the commandery was quiet.
translations.idiomatic: The southern chief Ruo'er had long raided the border; Zhu Bo won over his brothers, turned them double, ambushed Ruo'er, and restored quiet to the commandery.

sentences.zh: 徙為山陽太守，病免官。

translations.literal: He was transferred to governor of Shanyang and dismissed for illness.
translations.idiomatic: He moved to governor of Shanyang but resigned because of illness.

sentences.zh: 復徵為光祿大夫，遷廷尉，職典決疑，當言獻平天下獄。

translations.literal: He was again summoned as superintendent of the imperial household and promoted commandant of justice; his duty was to decide doubtful cases and submit even judgments on prisons under heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Recalled as superintendent of the imperial household, he became commandant of justice, charged with settling doubtful cases and harmonizing judgments empire-wide.

sentences.zh: 博恐為官屬所誣，視事，召見正監典法掾史，謂曰：「廷尉本起於武吏，不通法律，幸有眾賢，亦何憂！

translations.literal: Bo feared being slandered by his subordinates; when he took office he summoned the chief jailer and clerks of statutes and said, "The commandant of justice began as a military clerk and does not penetrate the laws; fortunately there are many worthies—what worry is there!
translations.idiomatic: Fearing his staff might deceive him, he told the chief jailers on day one: "I rose from the ranks and never memorized the code—luckily I have you experts.

sentences.zh: 然廷尉治郡斷獄以來且二十年，亦獨耳剽日久，三尺律令，人事出其中。

translations.literal: Yet since the commandant of justice has governed commanderies and decided cases for nearly twenty years, he has alone overheard long; the three-chi statutes and ordinances, human affairs emerge from within them.
translations.idiomatic: Yet I have run counties and courts for twenty years and picked up the gist: human affairs all fall within those statutes.

sentences.zh: 掾史試與正監共撰前世決事吏議難知者數十事，持以問廷尉，得為諸君覆意之。」

translations.literal: Clerk-secretaries, try together with the chief jailer to compile several dozen doubtful decisions and clerk deliberations from former ages that are hard to understand, bring them to ask the commandant of justice, and let him explain the intent for you all."
translations.idiomatic: Together draft a few dozen knotty precedents from old cases and quiz me on them—I will talk you through the reasoning."

sentences.zh: 正監以為博苟強，意未必能然，即共條白焉。

translations.literal: The chief jailer thought Bo was merely bluffing and probably could not do it; they immediately together itemized them.
translations.idiomatic: The jailers thought he was bluffing and drew up a long list.

sentences.zh: 博皆召掾史，並坐而問，為平處其輕重，十中八九。

translations.literal: Bo summoned all clerk-secretaries, sat with them, and asked; he balanced light and heavy for them and hit eight or nine out of ten.
translations.idiomatic: He called them in, weighed each problem, and scored eight or nine correct.

sentences.zh: 官屬咸服博之疏略，材過人也。

translations.literal: The official staff all admired Bo's broad grasp and talent surpassing others.
translations.idiomatic: The whole office marveled at his rough-hewn brilliance.

sentences.zh: 每遷徙易官，所到輒出奇譎如此，以明示下為不可欺者。

translations.literal: Whenever he was transferred or changed office, wherever he arrived he produced such strange cunning, thereby making plain to subordinates that he could not be deceived.
translations.idiomatic: At every new post he staged some such demonstration so no clerk dared think him a fool.

sentences.zh: 久之，遷後將軍，與紅陽侯立相善。

translations.literal: After a long time he was promoted rear general and was on good terms with Marquis of Hongyang, Liu Li.
translations.idiomatic: Promoted rear general, he grew close to Liu Li, Marquis of Hongyang.

sentences.zh: 立有罪就國，有司奏立黨友，博坐免。

translations.literal: Li had guilt and went to his fief; the proper offices memorialized Li's factional friends, and Bo by the case was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Li was sent to his fief for crimes, the authorities listed Zhu Bo among his partisans and cashiered him.

sentences.zh: 後歲餘，哀帝即位，以博名臣，召見，起家復為光祿大夫，遷為京兆尹，數月超為大司空。

translations.literal: More than a year later Emperor Ai ascended the throne; because Bo was a famous minister he was summoned, rose from commoner to be again superintendent of the imperial household, was promoted metropolitan governor, and within several months leaped to grand minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: A year later Emperor Ai took the throne, recalled Zhu Bo as a noted minister, restored him from private life to superintendent of the imperial household, then metropolitan governor, and within months to grand minister of works.

sentences.zh: 初，漢興襲秦官，置丞相、御史大夫、太尉。

translations.literal: At first, when Han rose it inherited Qin offices, setting chancellor, imperial secretary, and grand commandant.
translations.idiomatic: At the founding Han copied Qin by installing chancellor, imperial secretary, and grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 至武帝罷太尉，始置大司馬以冠將軍之號，非有印綬官屬也。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wu abolished the grand commandant, he first established grand marshal as a cap title for generals; it had no seal-ribbon or subordinate staff.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu dropped the grand commandant and added the title grand marshal as a general's honorific, without its own seal or staff.

sentences.zh: 及成帝時，何武為九卿，建言：「古者民樸事約，國之輔佐必得賢聖，然猶則天三光，備三公官，各有分職。

translations.literal: By Emperor Cheng's time He Wu was among the nine ministers and submitted advice: "In antiquity the people were simple and affairs few; the state's assistants had to be worthy sages, yet still they modeled heaven's three luminaries, fully providing the three duke offices, each with divided duties.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Cheng, Minister He Wu argued: "In high antiquity life was simple and government lean; even sage assistants modeled the three lights of heaven, so the three dukes each had a clear portfolio.

sentences.zh: 今末俗之弊，政事煩多，宰相之材不能及古，而丞相獨兼三公之事，所以久廢而不治也。

translations.literal: Now the flaws of decadent custom are many and affairs vexatious; the chancellor's talent cannot match antiquity, yet the chancellor alone combines the three dukes' business—therefore governance has long lain waste and is not ordered.
translations.idiomatic: Today business is tangled and no single chancellor can match the old ideal, yet one man still does three jobs—that is why government drags."

sentences.zh: 宜建三公官，定卿大夫之任，分職授政，以考功效。」

translations.literal: It is fitting to establish the three duke offices, fix the duties of ministers and grandees, divide offices and grant administration, and thereby examine merit and effect."
translations.idiomatic: He urged restoring the three dukes, defining ministers' roles, and splitting authority so performance could be judged."

sentences.zh: 其後上以問師安昌侯張禹，禹以為然。

translations.literal: Later the sovereign asked Tutor Marquis of Anchang Zhang Yu; Yu held it correct.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor consulted Tutor Zhang Yu of Anchang, who agreed.

sentences.zh: 時曲陽侯王根為大司馬票騎將軍，而何武為御史大夫。

translations.literal: At that time Marquis of Quyang Wang Gen was grand marshal and general-in-chief of agile cavalry, while He Wu was imperial secretary.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Gen, Marquis of Quyang, then held grand marshal with the agile-cavalry generalship, while He Wu was imperial secretary.

sentences.zh: 於是上賜曲陽侯根大司馬印綬，置官屬，罷票騎將軍官，以御史大夫何武為大司空，封列侯，皆增奉如丞相，以備三公官焉。

translations.literal: Thereupon the sovereign bestowed on Marquis of Quyang Gen the grand marshal seal and ribbon, established subordinate staff, abolished the agile-cavalry general office, made Imperial Secretary He Wu grand minister of works, enfeoffed him as marquis, and increased their salaries like the chancellor's, thereby completing the three duke offices.
translations.idiomatic: The throne gave Wang Gen the grand marshal seal and staff, dropped the separate agile-cavalry post, made He Wu grand minister of works with a marquisate and chancellor-level salary, rounding out the three excellencies.

sentences.zh: 議者多以為古今異制，漢自天下之號下至佐史皆不同於古，而獨改三公，職事難分明，無益於治亂。

translations.literal: Discussants mostly thought ancient and present institutions differed; Han from the title of all under heaven down to assistant clerks all differed from antiquity, yet to change only the three dukes made duties hard to separate clearly and brought no benefit to order or chaos.
translations.idiomatic: Critics complained that Han titles from the throne down to runners bore little resemblance to antiquity, so tinkering with the three excellencies only blurred lines of authority without helping order.

sentences.zh: 是時，御史府吏捨百餘區井水皆竭；

translations.literal: At that time in the censorate yamen more than a hundred clerk lodgings had wells whose water all ran dry;
translations.idiomatic: About then the censorate's hundred-odd clerk dormitories saw every well run dry;

sentences.zh: 又其府中列柏樹，常有野烏數千棲宿其上，晨去暮來，號日「朝夕烏」，烏去不來者數月，長老異之。

translations.literal: moreover in its courtyard stood a row of cypress trees on which several thousand wild crows habitually roosted, leaving at dawn and returning at dusk, called the "dawn-and-dusk crows"; the crows left and did not come for several months—the elders thought it strange.
translations.idiomatic: crows by the thousands roosted in the courtyard cypresses, flapping off at dawn and back at dusk until one day they vanished for months—old residents took it for an omen.

sentences.zh: 後二歲餘，硃博為大司空，奏言：「帝王之道不必相襲，各由時務。

translations.literal: More than two years later Zhu Bo became grand minister of works and submitted: "The way of emperors and kings need not copy one another; each follows the tasks of the time.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later Zhu Bo, as grand minister of works, argued that kings need not copy predecessors but adapt to their age.

sentences.zh: 高皇帝以聖德受命，建立鴻業，置御史大夫，位次丞相，典正法度，以職相參，總領百官，上下相監臨，歷載二百年，天下安寧。

translations.literal: The August Founder by sagely virtue received the mandate, set up the great enterprise, established the imperial secretary, rank next the chancellor, in charge of correcting law and standards, to participate in duties by office, oversee the hundred offices, and mutually supervise high and low—for two hundred years the realm has been peaceful.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu created the imperial secretary below the chancellor to guard the statutes, coordinate the bureaucracy, and keep checks on power—a system that kept peace for two hundred years.

sentences.zh: 今更為大司空，與丞相同位，未獲嘉祐。

translations.literal: Now it has been changed to grand minister of works, coequal with the chancellor, and auspicious blessing has not been obtained.
translations.idiomatic: Renaming that office grand minister of works and making it equal to the chancellor has brought no blessing.

sentences.zh: 故事，選郡國守相高第為中二千石，選中二千石為御史大夫，任職者為丞相，位次有序，所以尊聖德，重國相也。

translations.literal: By precedent the top-ranked commandery governors are chosen as fully two-thousand-dan; from fully two-thousand-dan the imperial secretary is chosen; the one who takes office becomes chancellor—the order of rank is sequential, thereby honoring sagely virtue and weighting the state's minister.
translations.idiomatic: Custom promoted star governors to full two-thousand-dan, then to imperial secretary, then to chancellor—a ladder that honored the realm's chief minister.

sentences.zh: 今中二千石未更御史大夫而為丞相，權輕，非所以重國政也。

translations.literal: Now fully two-thousand-dan men become chancellor without passing through imperial secretary; the power is light—not the way to weight state government.
translations.idiomatic: Skipping the censorate on the way to chancellor weakens the chain of command.

sentences.zh: 臣愚以為大司空官可罷，復置御史大夫，遵奉舊制。

translations.literal: Your servant foolishly thinks the grand minister of works office may be abolished and the imperial secretary restored, following the old system.
translations.idiomatic: I move to abolish grand minister of works and restore the imperial secretary under the old rules.

sentences.zh: 臣願盡力，以御史大夫為百僚率。」

translations.literal: Your servant wishes to exert his strength to the utmost, taking the imperial secretary as leader of the hundred offices."
translations.idiomatic: I will serve with all I have as first among the ministers."

sentences.zh: 哀帝從之，乃更拜博為御史大夫。

translations.literal: Emperor Ai followed it; then he again appointed Bo imperial secretary.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ai agreed and named Zhu Bo imperial secretary.

sentences.zh: 會大司馬喜免，以陽安侯丁明為大司馬衛將軍，置官屬，大司馬冠號如故事。

translations.literal: It happened that Grand Marshal Xi was dismissed; Marquis of Yang'an Ding Ming was made grand marshal and guards general with subordinate staff; the grand marshal cap title followed old precedent.
translations.idiomatic: When Grand Marshal Fu Xi fell, Ding Ming became grand marshal and guards general with full staff, reviving the old grand marshal style.

sentences.zh: 後四歲，哀帝遂改丞相為大司徒，復置大司空、大司馬焉。

translations.literal: Four years later Emperor Ai changed the chancellor to grand tutor, and again set grand minister of works and grand marshal.
translations.idiomatic: Four years on, Ai renamed the chancellor grand tutor and reinstated grand minister of works and grand marshal.

sentences.zh: 初，何武為大司空，又與丞相方進共奏言：「古選諸侯賢者以為州伯，《書》曰『咨十有二牧』，所以廣聰明，燭幽隱也。

translations.literal: At first, when He Wu was grand minister of works, he together with Chancellor Fangjin also submitted: "In antiquity worthy regional lords were chosen as shepherds of the provinces; the Documents says, 'Consult the twelve pastors'—thereby broadening clear hearing and lighting what is hidden.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, as grand minister of works, He Wu and Chancellor Zhai Fangjin had memorialized that ancient kings picked worthy lords as regional shepherds—the Documents speaks of twelve pastors—to widen the court's ears and expose hidden wrong.

sentences.zh: 今部刺史居牧伯之位，秉一州之統，選第大吏，所薦位高至九卿，所惡立退，任重職大。

translations.literal: Now regional inspectors hold shepherds' seats, grasp a province's lines, choose and rank great clerks; those they recommend reach as high as the nine ministers, those they hate are immediately dismissed—the burden is weighty and the office great.
translations.idiomatic: Today's regional inspectors wield a pastor's power, pick senior officials, and can vault protégés to the nine ministers or ruin careers at a stroke.

sentences.zh: 《春秋》之義，用貴治賤，不以卑臨尊。

translations.literal: The meaning of the Spring and Autumn is: use the noble to govern the base; do not let the base oversee the noble.
translations.idiomatic: The Annals teach that the high should discipline the low, not the reverse.

sentences.zh: 刺史位下大夫，而臨二千石，輕重不相準，失位次之序。

translations.literal: Inspectors rank below grandees yet oversee two-thousand-dan men—the weight is not balanced and the order of stations is lost.
translations.idiomatic: Yet an inspector ranks only with a grandee while judging two-thousand-dan magistrates—a mismatch in rank.

sentences.zh: 臣請罷刺史，更置州牧，以應古制。」

translations.literal: Your servant requests to abolish regional inspectors and instead set provincial shepherds, to accord with ancient institutions."
translations.idiomatic: We ask to replace inspectors with provincial shepherds, as in antiquity."

sentences.zh: 奏可。

translations.literal: The memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The throne approved.

sentences.zh: 及博奏復御史大夫官，又奏言：「漢家至德溥大，宇內萬里，立置郡縣。

translations.literal: When Bo memorialized to restore the imperial secretary, he also submitted: "The house of Han's supreme virtue spreads broadly; within the realm of ten thousand li it has established commanderies and counties.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhu Bo restored the censorate, he added: "Han's virtue fills the realm of commanderies and counties.

sentences.zh: 部刺史奉使典州，督察郡國，吏民安寧。

translations.literal: Regional inspectors, bearing commission, govern provinces, supervise commanderies and kingdoms, and clerks and people are peaceful and quiet.
translations.idiomatic: Inspectors hold imperial warrant, watch the commanderies, and keep officials and people at peace.

sentences.zh: 故事，居部九歲舉為守相，其有異材功效著者輒登擢，秩卑而賞厚，咸勸功樂進。

translations.literal: By precedent, after nine years in a circuit one is promoted governor; those of unusual talent and manifest merit are promptly elevated—rank is low but reward is thick, so all are urged toward merit and gladly advance.
translations.idiomatic: Custom kept them in a circuit nine years before promoting the best; rank stayed modest but rewards were rich, so men strove to excel.

sentences.zh: 前丞相方進奏罷刺史，更置州牧，秩真二千石，位次九卿。

translations.literal: Former Chancellor Fangjin memorialized to abolish regional inspectors and set provincial shepherds at true two-thousand-dan, rank next the nine ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Zhai Fangjin had replaced them with provincial shepherds at full two-thousand-dan, just below the nine ministers.

sentences.zh: 九卿缺，以高第補，其中材則苟自守而已，恐功效陵夷，奸軌不禁。

translations.literal: When nine ministers were short, the top men filled them; those of middling talent merely guarded themselves—We fear merit will slip, villainy unchecked.
translations.idiomatic: Mediocre shepherds simply guarded their seats while talent dried up and crime spread.

sentences.zh: 臣請罷州牧，置刺史如故。」

translations.literal: Your servant requests to abolish provincial shepherds and set regional inspectors as before."
translations.idiomatic: I ask to dismiss the shepherds and return to regional inspectors."

sentences.zh: 奏可。

translations.literal: The memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor agreed again.

sentences.zh: 博為人廉儉，不好酒色游宴。

translations.literal: Bo as a person was incorrupt and frugal; he did not love wine, sex, or roaming feasts.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo lived plainly and shunned carousing.

sentences.zh: 自微賤至富貴，食不重味，案上不過三懷，夜寢早起，妻希見其面。

translations.literal: From lowly to noble he did not double flavors at meals; on his desk there were no more than three cups; he retired late and rose early, and his wife rarely saw his face.
translations.idiomatic: From poverty to power he never doubled dishes at a meal, kept only three cups on his desk, worked from before dawn until late at night, and his wife rarely saw him.

sentences.zh: 有一女，無男。

translations.literal: He had one daughter, no sons.
translations.idiomatic: He had a daughter but no son.

sentences.zh: 然好樂士大夫，為郡守九卿，賓客滿門，欲仕宦者薦舉之，欲報仇怨者解劍以帶之。

translations.literal: Yet he loved to delight gentlemen; as commandery governor or nine minister his gate was full of guests—those who wished office he recommended and raised; those who wished to repay grudges he unbuckled his sword and gave them.
translations.idiomatic: Yet he courted scholars: as governor or minister his house thronged with guests—he sponsored the ambitious and lent his sword to men seeking revenge.

sentences.zh: 其趨事待士如是，博以此自立，然終用敗。

translations.literal: His rushing to affairs and treating gentlemen was thus; Bo thereby established himself, yet in the end was ruined by it.
translations.idiomatic: He built a career on hustle and patronage—and in the end it destroyed him.

sentences.zh: 初，哀帝祖母定陶太后欲求稱尊號，太后從弟高武侯傅喜為大司馬，與丞相孔光、大司空師丹共持正議。

translations.literal: At first, Emperor Ai's grandmother the Dowager of Dingtao wished to seek an honored title; the dowager's cousin Marquis of Gaowu Fu Xi was grand marshal and together with Chancellor Kong Guang and Grand Minister of Works Shi Dan upheld correct deliberation.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Ai's grandmother, the Dowager of Dingtao, demanded an imperial-style title, her cousin Fu Xi as grand marshal stood with Kong Guang and Shi Dan on the side of precedent.

sentences.zh: 孔鄉侯傅晏亦太后從弟，諂諛欲順指，會博新徵用為京兆尹，與交結，謀成尊號，以廣孝道。

translations.literal: Marquis of Kongxiang Fu Yan was also the dowager's cousin; he flattered and wished to follow her finger; it happened that Bo had newly been summoned as metropolitan governor and he formed a tie with Yan, plotting to complete the honored title so as to broaden filial devotion.
translations.idiomatic: Her cousin Fu Yan of Kongxiang toadied to her; Zhu Bo, newly named metropolitan governor, allied with Yan to push the title through in the name of filial piety.

sentences.zh: 由是師丹先免，博代為大司空，數燕見奏封事，言：「丞相光志在自守，不能憂國；

translations.literal: Thereupon Shi Dan was dismissed first; Bo replaced him as grand minister of works; several times at banquets he submitted sealed matters, saying, "Chancellor Kong Guang's intent is to guard himself and cannot worry for the state;
translations.idiomatic: Shi Dan was dismissed first; Zhu Bo succeeded him as grand minister of works and repeatedly at informal audiences filed sealed memorials claiming that Chancellor Kong Guang thought only of self-preservation and would not put the state first;

sentences.zh: 大司馬喜至尊至親，阿黨大臣，無益政治。」

translations.literal: the grand marshal Xi is the most honored and closest kin, yet he toadies to great ministers—no benefit to government."
translations.idiomatic: that Grand Marshal Fu Xi was the sovereign's nearest kinsman yet curried favor with powerful ministers, to the harm of government."

sentences.zh: 上遂罷喜遣就國，免光為庶人，以博代光為丞相，封陽鄉侯，食邑二千戶。

translations.literal: The sovereign thereupon dismissed Xi and sent him to his fief, dismissed Guang to commoner status, made Bo replace Guang as chancellor, enfeoffed him as Marquis of Yangxiang, and granted a fief of two thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor banished Fu Xi to his fief, reduced Kong Guang to commoner rank, named Zhu Bo chancellor in his place, enfeoffed him as Marquis of Yangxiang with two thousand households of income.

sentences.zh: 博上書讓曰：「故事封丞相不滿千戶，而獨臣過制，誠慚懼，願還千戶。」

translations.literal: Bo submitted a memorial declining, saying, "By precedent a chancellor's enfeoffment is less than a thousand households, yet Your servant alone exceeds the regulation—truly ashamed and fearful; I beg to return a thousand households."
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo memorialized in protest: "Chancellors have never held more than a thousand-household fiefs; mine breaks precedent and shames me—I ask to give back one thousand households."

sentences.zh: 上許焉。

translations.literal: The sovereign granted it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor allowed it.

sentences.zh: 傅太后怨傅喜不已，使孔鄉侯晏風丞相，令奏免喜侯。

translations.literal: The Fu empress dowager endlessly resented Fu Xi and had Marquis of Kongxiang Yan hint to the chancellor, ordering him to memorialize to strip Xi of his marquisate.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Empress Dowager Fu still hated Fu Xi and sent Fu Yan to lean on the chancellor to memorialize for stripping Fu Xi of his title.

sentences.zh: 博受詔，與御史大夫趙玄議，玄言：「事已前決，得無不宜？」

translations.literal: Bo received the edict and deliberated with Imperial Secretary Zhao Xuan; Xuan said, "The matter was already decided earlier—might it not be inappropriate?"
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo took the order and consulted Imperial Secretary Zhao Xuan, who asked, "The case was settled long ago—is it wise to reopen it?"

sentences.zh: 博曰：「已許孔鄉侯有指。

translations.literal: Bo said, "We already promised Marquis of Kongxiang a commitment.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo replied, "I gave Fu Yan my word."

sentences.zh: 匹夫相要，尚相得死，何況至尊？

translations.literal: When commoners make mutual demands, they may still die for one another—how much more the most honored!"
translations.idiomatic: Commoners keep a blood oath to the death—how can I refuse the grand empress dowager?"

sentences.zh: 博唯有死耳！」

translations.literal: Bo has only death!"
translations.idiomatic: I would rather die than break faith!"

sentences.zh: 玄即許可。

translations.literal: Xuan immediately granted assent.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Xuan yielded.

sentences.zh: 博惡獨斥奏喜，以故大司空汜鄉侯何武前亦坐過免就國，事與喜相似，即並奏：「喜、武前在位，皆無益於治，雖已退免，爵士之封非所當得也。

translations.literal: Bo hated to impeach Xi alone; because the former grand minister of works Marquis of Sìxiang He Wu had earlier also sat for an offense, been dismissed, and gone to his fief, the affair being similar to Xi's, he therefore memorialized together: "Xi and Wu, while previously in office, were both of no benefit to governance; although already dismissed and removed, enfeoffment of noble rank and fief is not what they ought to obtain.
translations.idiomatic: Unwilling to single out Fu Xi alone, Zhu Bo lumped in He Wu, former grand minister of works and Marquis of Sìxiang, who had also been cashiered to his fief: "Neither Fu Xi nor He Wu helped the government while in power; though removed, they should not keep their marquisates.

sentences.zh: 請皆免為庶人。」

translations.literal: We request that both be dismissed to commoner status."
translations.idiomatic: Strip both to commoner rank."

sentences.zh: 上知傅太后素常怨喜，疑博、玄承指，即召玄詣尚書問狀。

translations.literal: The sovereign knew the Fu empress dowager had always resented Xi; he suspected Bo and Xuan carried out her hint; he immediately summoned Xuan to the secretariat to question the circumstances.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor knew the grand empress dowager's grudge against Fu Xi and suspected Zhu Bo and Zhao Xuan acted on her nod; he summoned Zhao Xuan to the secretariat for questioning.

sentences.zh: 玄辭服，有詔左將軍彭宣與中朝者雜問。

translations.literal: Xuan confessed and submitted; there was an edict for General of the Left Peng Xuan and middle-court officials jointly to interrogate.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Xuan confessed. The throne ordered General of the Left Peng Xuan and inner-court officials to conduct a joint hearing.

sentences.zh: 宣等劾奏：「博宰相，玄上卿，晏以外親封位特進，股肱大臣，上所信任，不思竭誠奉公，務廣恩化，為百寮先，皆知喜、武前已蒙恩詔決，事更三赦，博執正道，虧損上恩，以結信貴戚，背君鄉臣，傾亂政治，奸人之雄，附下罔上，為臣不忠不道；

translations.literal: Xuan and others impeached: "Bo is chancellor, Xuan is upper minister, Yan by outer kinship holds specially advanced rank—they are the arms and thighs the sovereign trusts, yet they do not think to exhaust loyalty and serve the public, widen kindness and transformation, and take the lead for the hundred offices; they all knew Xi and Wu had already received merciful edicts and the matter had passed through three amnesties; Bo abandoning the straight path injures superior favor to knot trust with noble in-laws, turns his back on the ruler and faces the ministers, overturns and disorders government, is chief among villains, attaches below and deceives above—as a minister he is disloyal and not in accord with the Way;
translations.idiomatic: Peng Xuan and colleagues charged: "Zhu Bo and Zhao Xuan are pillars of state yet curried favor with the Fu clan, betrayed clear imperial mercy already confirmed through three amnesties, and overturned policy to please in-laws—disloyal and lawless conduct;

sentences.zh: 玄知博所言非法，枉義附從，大不敬；

translations.literal: Xuan knew Bo's words were not lawful, yet bent righteousness to follow—great irreverence;
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Xuan knew the memorial was unlawful yet went along—gross irreverence;

sentences.zh: 晏與博議免喜，失禮不敬。

translations.literal: Yan plotted with Bo to dismiss Xi—loss of ritual and irreverence.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Yan conspired with Zhu Bo to ruin Fu Xi—another breach of propriety.

sentences.zh: 臣請詔謁者召博、玄、晏詣廷尉詔獄。」

translations.literal: Your servant requests that an edict be issued to the usher summoning Bo, Xuan, and Yan to the commandant of justice's imperial prison."
translations.idiomatic: We ask that Zhu Bo, Zhao Xuan, and Fu Yan be taken to the imperial prison under the commandant of justice."

sentences.zh: 制曰：「將軍、中二千石、二千石、諸大夫、博士、議郎議。」

translations.literal: A rescript said: "Let generals, fully two-thousand-dan, two-thousand-dan, grandees, erudits, and deliberation gentlemen deliberate."
translations.idiomatic: An edict called in ministers and advisers for debate.

sentences.zh: 右將軍蟜望等四十四人以為：「如宣等言，可許。」

translations.literal: General of the Right Wang Jiao and forty-four others held: "As Xuan and the others say—it may be permitted."
translations.idiomatic: General of the Right Wang Jiao and forty-four others agreed with Peng Xuan's memorial and urged the throne to accept it.

sentences.zh: 諫大夫龔勝等十四人以為：「《春秋》之義，奸以事君，常刑不捨。

translations.literal: Remonstrance grandee Gong Sheng and fourteen others held: "The meaning of the Spring and Autumn is: treachery in serving the ruler—ordinary punishment does not spare.
translations.idiomatic: Remonstrance grandee Gong Sheng and fourteen others cited the Annals: treason against the ruler admits no ordinary mercy;

sentences.zh: 魯大夫叔孫僑如欲顓公室，譖其族兄季孫行父於晉，晉執囚行父以亂魯國，《春秋》重而書之。

translations.literal: Lu grandee Sun Qiaoru wished to monopolize the ducal house and slandered his agnate elder Xingfu to Jin; Jin seized and imprisoned Xingfu, disordering Lu—the Spring and Autumn thought it weighty and recorded it.
translations.idiomatic: when Sun Qiaoru of Lu slandered his kinsman Ji Xingfu to Jin and threw Lu into chaos, the Annals condemned it harshly.

sentences.zh: 今晏放命圯族，干亂朝政，要大臣以罔上，本造計謀，職為亂階，宜與博、玄同罪，罪皆不道。」

translations.literal: Now Yan abandoned commands and ruined the clan, disturbed court government, coerced great ministers to deceive the sovereign, was originator of the plot, and his office was the step of disorder—he should share guilt with Bo and Xuan; all their crimes are not in accord with the Way."
translations.idiomatic: Fu Yan defied the throne, stirred the court, extorted senior ministers, and hatched the plot—he should share Zhu Bo and Zhao Xuan's guilt; all three are guilty of capital disloyalty."

sentences.zh: 上減玄死罪三等，削晏戶四分之一，假謁者節召丞相詣廷尉詔獄。

translations.literal: The sovereign reduced Xuan's death guilt three degrees, cut a quarter of Yan's household income, lent the usher's staff to summon the chancellor to the commandant of justice's imperial prison.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor commuted Zhao Xuan's death sentence three steps, trimmed Fu Yan's fief by a quarter, and sent the usher with credentials to take Zhu Bo into the imperial prison.

sentences.zh: 博自殺，國除。

translations.literal: Bo killed himself; the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo committed suicide and his marquisate was extinguished.

sentences.zh: 初，博以御史為丞相，封陽鄉侯，玄以少府為御史大夫，並拜於前殿，廷登受策，有音如鐘聲。

translations.literal: At first, when Bo went from imperial secretary to chancellor and was enfeoffed Marquis of Yangxiang, and Xuan went from privy treasurer to imperial secretary, they were jointly invested in the front hall; as they ascended the court steps to receive the edicts there was a sound like a bell.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhu Bo rose from imperial secretary to Marquis of Yangxiang chancellor and Zhao Xuan from privy treasurer to imperial secretary, both were invested in the front hall; as they mounted the steps to receive their patents a bell-like tone rang out.

sentences.zh: 語在《五行志》。

translations.literal: The account is in the "Treatise on the Five Phases."
translations.idiomatic: The omen is recorded in the treatise on the Five Phases.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：薛宣、硃博皆起佐史，歷位以登宰相。

translations.literal: The appraiser says: Xue Xuan and Zhu Bo both rose from assistant clerks and ascended step by step to chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: The historian concludes: Xue Xuan and Zhu Bo climbed from petty clerks to the chancellorship.

sentences.zh: 宣所在而治，為世吏師，及居大位，以苛察失名，器誠有極也。

translations.literal: Wherever Xuan served was well governed; he became the age's model clerk, yet when he reached high position he lost reputation through harsh scrutiny—capacity truly has its limit.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan governed every post well and taught a generation how to administer, but at the summit his nit-picking cost him his good name—talent has its ceiling.

sentences.zh: 博馳聘進取，不思道德，已亡可言，又見孝成之世委任大臣，假借用權。

translations.literal: Bo galloped in pursuit of advancement, did not think on virtue and the Way—already nothing worth saying; moreover he saw that in Emperor Cheng's reign great ministers were entrusted and borrowed authority.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo chased power without scruple and, seeing how Emperor Cheng's great ministers had wielded borrowed authority, mistook the lesson.

sentences.zh: 世主已更，好惡異前，復附丁、傅稱順孔鄉。

translations.literal: The age's ruler had changed; likes and dislikes differed from before; again he attached to Ding and Fu and called compliance with Marquis of Kongxiang.
translations.idiomatic: A new emperor brought new tastes; Zhu Bo hitched himself to the Ding and Fu clans and echoed Fu Yan.

sentences.zh: 事發見詰，遂陷誣罔，辭窮情得，仰藥飲鳩。

translations.literal: When the affair came to light he was questioned, then fell into false accusation; words exhausted and facts obtained, he swallowed poison and drank aconite.
translations.idiomatic: Exposed and cornered, he drank poison and swallowed aconite.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「久矣哉，由之行詐也！」

translations.literal: Confucius said, "How long it has been—You's conduct is deceitful!"
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said of Zilu, "His tricks have gone on far too long!"

sentences.zh: 博亦然哉！

translations.literal: Was not Bo likewise!
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo was no different.
